## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBABI

## Character

Mechanical: a project-wide Dockerfile-syntax constraint, factored into its own BCG library module so kludge and conjure paths share one rule, plus the BCG cleanups in `rbfk_kludge` that BBABH's pristine-state work accumulated.

## Mint

`rbfh` — FoundryHygiene. Library module `Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` (no CLI — utility module per BCG). Public function `rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>` — dies on violation, returns normally on pass. Predicate `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` stays in `rbfk_kludge.sh`; image-presence is not a hygiene concern.

## Hygiene rules

Applied to every line at column 0 starting with `FROM` followed by whitespace, after `#`-column-0 comments are filtered out. Violation → `buc_die` with `{path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line}` precision.

1. No tab character anywhere on the FROM line
2. Second whitespace-delimited token is exactly `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`, or `scratch` (lowercase only)
3. Line does not end with `\`

Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible — the rule constrains token 2 only. Empty Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently; Docker handles its own rejection.

## Callsites

- `rbfk_kludge` in `rbfk_kludge.sh` — after slot resolution, before `docker build`. Checks `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`.
- `rbfd_build` in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — immediately after the existing `test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"` validation, before Cloud Build stitching begins.

Out of scope: `rbfd_graft` — `RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE` is SLSA recipe metadata only, read by `rbfv` for display; graft itself builds from `RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE` (pre-built). Synthesized scratch+COPY Dockerfiles in `rbfd_push_build_context` and Cloud Build pod step scripts (`rbgjv*`, `rbgjb*`, `rbgja*`) are project-controlled, not user-authored.

Each consumer CLI (`rbfk_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`) sources `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` and calls `zrbfh_kindle` in its furnish.

## BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge (BBABH debt)

- Multi-var `local` declarations split one-per-line; `local -r` where assign-once
- `test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0` → `(( ${#z_build_args[@]} ))`
- Extract `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` consolidating the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites; stderr routes to a counter-discriminated forensic temp file under a new `ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX` kindle constant with `z_rbfk_inspect_counter` mutable kindle state.

## Vessel remediation

`rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` currently hardcodes `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` — the exact bypass the hygiene check exists to catch. Declare the upstream via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` in the vessel's `rbrv.env` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing); rewrite Dockerfile head to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`. Lands alongside the hygiene module so the gauntlet remains runnable end-to-end.

## Spec

New subdoc `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` — canonical rule home, follow RBSDS-dns_step shape: short, MCM-annotated (`//axhob_operation` + `//axhos_step` per rule), `{quoin}` references for linked terms. Mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene` for cross-referencing.

- RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with a `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin reference.
- RBSAC ark_conjure — add reference at the build dispatch step.

Update CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: add `RBFH` (module) and `RBSFH` (spec subdoc) entries to the RBK subdirectory section.

Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBABI

## Character

Mechanical: a project-wide Dockerfile-syntax constraint, factored into its own BCG library module so kludge and conjure paths share one rule, plus the BCG cleanups in `rbfk_kludge` that BBABH's pristine-state work accumulated.

## Mint

`rbfh` — FoundryHygiene. Library module `Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` (no CLI — utility module per BCG). Public function `rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>` — dies on violation, returns normally on pass. Predicate `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` stays in `rbfk_kludge.sh`; image-presence is not a hygiene concern.

## Hygiene rules

Applied to every line at column 0 starting with `FROM` followed by whitespace, after `#`-column-0 comments are filtered out. Violation → `buc_die` with `{path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line}` precision.

1. No tab character anywhere on the FROM line
2. Second whitespace-delimited token is exactly `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`, or `scratch` (lowercase only)
3. Line does not end with `\`

Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible — the rule constrains token 2 only. Empty Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently; Docker handles its own rejection.

## Callsites

- `rbfk_kludge` in `rbfk_kludge.sh` — after slot resolution, before `docker build`. Checks `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`.
- `rbfd_build` in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — immediately after the existing `test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"` validation, before Cloud Build stitching begins.

Out of scope: `rbfd_graft` — `RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE` is SLSA recipe metadata only, read by `rbfv` for display; graft itself builds from `RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE` (pre-built). Synthesized scratch+COPY Dockerfiles in `rbfd_push_build_context` and Cloud Build pod step scripts (`rbgjv*`, `rbgjb*`, `rbgja*`) are project-controlled, not user-authored.

Each consumer CLI (`rbfk_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`) sources `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` and calls `zrbfh_kindle` in its furnish.

## BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge (BBABH debt)

- Multi-var `local` declarations split one-per-line; `local -r` where assign-once
- `test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0` → `(( ${#z_build_args[@]} ))`
- Extract `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` consolidating the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites; stderr routes to a counter-discriminated forensic temp file under a new `ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX` kindle constant with `z_rbfk_inspect_counter` mutable kindle state.

## Vessel remediation

`rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` currently hardcodes `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` — the exact bypass the hygiene check exists to catch. Declare the upstream via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` in the vessel's `rbrv.env` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing); rewrite Dockerfile head to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`. Lands alongside the hygiene module so the gauntlet remains runnable end-to-end.

## Spec

New subdoc `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` — canonical rule home, follow RBSDS-dns_step shape: short, MCM-annotated (`//axhob_operation` + `//axhos_step` per rule), `{quoin}` references for linked terms. Mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene` for cross-referencing.

- RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with a `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin reference.
- RBSAC ark_conjure — add reference at the build dispatch step.

Update CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: add `RBFH` (module) and `RBSFH` (spec subdoc) entries to the RBK subdirectory section.

content.text:
**Pace goal:** Mint a new `rbfh` BCG library module enforcing Dockerfile `FROM` syntax (three rules) at both `rbfk_kludge` and `rbfd_build` callsites, fold in BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in `rbfk_kludge`, remediate the one vessel that bypasses `RBF_IMAGE_n`, and write the spec subdoc that anchors the rule.

**Heat:** `rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification` (₣BB, racing, 52/61) — paddock shapes the gauntlet/pristine-tier work; this pace is the residual BBABH chivvy: closing the hardcoded-FROM bypass that `--pull=never` used to backstop before buildx made the flag a parse error.

**Recent context:** BBABH split rbfk from rbfd (kludge decoupled from director credentials), then empirically discovered `--pull=never` was already dead on buildx. The hygiene check exists as the substitute defense, chivvied here. Last commit `ea949e6f` bumped tadmor's sentry hallmark on the post-decoupled rbfk.

**Docket name assessment:** silks `kludge-dockerfile-from-hygiene` no longer fits — the docket itself proposes rename since work is now project-wide with kludge as one of two callsites. The docket suggests `foundry-dockerfile-hygiene-module`.

**Full docket** *(distilled — full text in gazette)*:

1. **Module mint** — `Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`, public `rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>` (dies on violation). No CLI.
2. **Three FROM-line rules** — no tabs; token 2 ∈ `${RBF_IMAGE_1..3}` | `scratch`; no trailing `\`. Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas admissible. Empty/no-FROM passes silently.
3. **Two callsites** — `rbfk_kludge` (after slot resolution, before `docker build`) and `rbfd_build` (after the existing `test -f` validation, before Cloud Build stitching).
4. **BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge** (BBABH debt) — split multi-var locals, `(( ${#arr[@]} ))` over `test -gt 0`, extract `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` from the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites with counter-discriminated forensic stderr.
5. **Vessel remediation** — `rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter` currently hardcodes `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm`. Declare via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel), rewrite Dockerfile to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`.
6. **Spec** — new `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` (RBSDS shape, MCM-annotated, mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene`). Update RBSAK Step 6 to reference it, RBSAC at build dispatch, plus CLAUDE.md acronym mappings for RBFH/RBSFH.
7. **Verify** — fast suite green, pristine kludge sentry completes through hallmark publication, hygiene check passes on conjure path, jupyter vessel passes post-remediation, `bash -n` clean.

**Approach:**
- New module + spec subdoc + acronym mappings can land first (additive, no callsite risk).
- Wire callsites next (two surgical insertions).
- rbfk_kludge BCG cleanups + predicate extraction in same change.
- Vessel remediation last (independent — verify the rbrv.env field name empirically before editing).
- Run fast suite after each meaningful slice; pristine kludge sentry once everything's wired.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — multi-file with cross-file coherence (spec ↔ code ↔ CLAUDE.md), BCG patterns matter, and the vessel remediation needs empirical verification against a working rbrv.env. No parallelization opportunity.

Three things to confirm before I begin:

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge - local image build for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR, no credentials)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBFK_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBFK_SOURCED=1
26	
27	# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
28	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)
32	
33	zrbfk_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"
35	
36	  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
37	  zrbfc_kindle
38	
39	  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
40	}
41	
42	zrbfk_sentinel() {
43	  zrbfc_sentinel
44	  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
45	}
46	
47	######################################################################
48	# External Functions (rbfk_*)
49	
50	######################################################################
51	# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
52	#
53	# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
54	# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
55	# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
56	# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
57	#
58	# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
59	# Host platform only (no multi-arch).
60	
61	rbfk_kludge() {
62	  zrbfk_sentinel
63	
64	  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
65	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
66	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
67	
68	  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
69	  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
70	  git diff --quiet \
71	    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
72	  git diff --cached --quiet \
73	    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"
74	
75	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
76	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
77	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
78	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
79	
80	  # Load vessel configuration
81	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
82	
83	  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
84	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
85	    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
86	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
87	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
88	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
89	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
90	  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
91	    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
92	  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
93	    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"
94	
95	  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
96	  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
97	  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
98	  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
99	  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
100	  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
101	  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
102	  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
103	  local z_build_args=()
104	  local z_slot="" z_origin_var="" z_anchor_var="" z_origin="" z_anchor=""
105	  local z_slot_ref="" z_pkg_path="" z_tag="" z_miss=""
106	  local z_anchored_misses=()
107	  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
108	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
109	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
110	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
111	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
112	
113	    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
114	      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
115	    fi
116	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
117	
118	    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
119	      case "${z_anchor}" in
120	        *:*) : ;;
121	        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
122	      esac
123	      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
124	      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
125	      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
126	      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
127	      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
128	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
129	      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
130	        z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
131	      fi
132	    else
133	      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
134	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
135	      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
136	        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
137	        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
138	          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
139	        docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
140	          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
141	      fi
142	    fi
143	
144	    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
145	  done
146	  test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0 || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"
147	
148	  if test "${#z_anchored_misses[@]}" -gt 0; then
149	    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
150	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
151	    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
152	    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
153	    buc_bare ""
154	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
155	    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
156	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
157	    done
158	    buc_bare ""
159	    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
160	  fi
161	
162	  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
163	  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
164	  local -r z_hallmark="${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"
165	
166	  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
167	  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
168	  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
169	
170	  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
171	  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
172	  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"
173	
174	  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
175	  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
176	  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
177	  buc_step "Building image locally"
178	  docker build \
179	    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
180	    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
181	    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
182	    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
183	    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
184	
185	  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
186	  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
187	  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
188	    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"
189	
190	  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
191	  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
192	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
193	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
194	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"
195	
196	  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
197	  buc_bare ""
198	  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
199	  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
200	  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
201	  buc_bare ""
202	  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
203	}

205	# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge - local image build for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR, no credentials)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFK_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFK_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)

zrbfk_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfk_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfk_*)

######################################################################
# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
#
# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
#
# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

rbfk_kludge() {
  zrbfk_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Load vessel configuration
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"

  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_build_args=()
  local z_slot="" z_origin_var="" z_anchor_var="" z_origin="" z_anchor=""
  local z_slot_ref="" z_pkg_path="" z_tag="" z_miss=""
  local z_anchored_misses=()
  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
    fi
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
      fi
    else
      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
      fi
    fi

    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
  done
  test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0 || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

  if test "${#z_anchored_misses[@]}" -gt 0; then
    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
    buc_bare ""
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
    done
    buc_bare ""
    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
  fi

  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
  local -r z_hallmark="${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"

  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"

  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
  buc_step "Building image locally"
  docker build \
    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"

  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfk_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfk_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfk_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge" zrbfk_furnish "$@"

1	//axhob_operation
2	{scr_dns_step}
3	
4	Configures sentry DNS resolution via `/etc/resolv.conf` pointing to {rbrr_dns_server}.
5	
6	If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is disabled, the {at_sentry_container}:
7	
8	* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from the interface attached to {at_enclave_network} (UDP and TCP port 53)
9	* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to the interface attached to {at_transit_network} (UDP and TCP port 53)
10	
11	If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global or allowlist, the {at_sentry_container} executes this sequence:
12	
13	//axhos_step
14	. Dnsmasq Configuration:
15	+
16	--
17	* Core Settings:
18	** Bind strictly to the interface attached to {at_enclave_network}
19	** Listen on {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
20	** DNS service only — all DHCP functionality disabled (no-dhcp-interface)
21	** Isolated from host resolver (no-resolv)
22	** Exclude the interface attached to {at_transit_network} from listening (except-interface)
23	** Security: bogus-priv, domain-needed, strict-order
24	** Forwarding concurrency: dns-forward-max=150
25	** Source port range: 4096-65535
26	
27	* Cache Settings:
28	** Cache size: 1000 entries
29	** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
30	** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
31	
32	* Logging (all modes):
33	** log-queries=extra
34	** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
35	** log-dhcp, log-debug, log-async=20
36	
37	* Query Control:
38	** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global:
39	*** Forward all queries to {rbrr_dns_server}
40	*** Return all responses unmodified
41	
42	** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is allowlist:
43	*** Resolve-then-freeze: for each domain in {rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}, resolve A records via {rbrr_dns_server} at startup, then freeze as static `address=` entries in dnsmasq. No `server=` forwarding directives — dnsmasq synthesizes responses locally from frozen IPs. Subdomain queries (e.g., `exfil.anthropic.com`) return the frozen parent IP without generating upstream queries, closing the DNS subdomain exfiltration channel.
44	*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries (address=/#/)
45	--
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. Service Launch:
49	* Start dnsmasq daemon
50	* Wait for initialization (2 second delay)
51	
52	//axhos_step
53	. Filter Rules:
54	+
55	--
56	* Accept DNS queries arriving at sentry from enclave:
57	** RBM-INGRESS: interface attached to {at_enclave_network}, UDP port 53
58	** RBM-INGRESS: interface attached to {at_enclave_network}, TCP port 53
59	* Accept sentry upstream DNS forwarding:
60	** RBM-EGRESS: interface attached to {at_transit_network}, UDP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
61	** RBM-EGRESS: interface attached to {at_transit_network}, TCP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
62	--
63

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{scr_dns_step}

Configures sentry DNS resolution via `/etc/resolv.conf` pointing to {rbrr_dns_server}.

If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is disabled, the {at_sentry_container}:

* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from the interface attached to {at_enclave_network} (UDP and TCP port 53)
* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to the interface attached to {at_transit_network} (UDP and TCP port 53)

If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global or allowlist, the {at_sentry_container} executes this sequence:

//axhos_step
. Dnsmasq Configuration:
+
--
* Core Settings:
** Bind strictly to the interface attached to {at_enclave_network}
** Listen on {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
** DNS service only — all DHCP functionality disabled (no-dhcp-interface)
** Isolated from host resolver (no-resolv)
** Exclude the interface attached to {at_transit_network} from listening (except-interface)
** Security: bogus-priv, domain-needed, strict-order
** Forwarding concurrency: dns-forward-max=150
** Source port range: 4096-65535

* Cache Settings:
** Cache size: 1000 entries
** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds

* Logging (all modes):
** log-queries=extra
** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
** log-dhcp, log-debug, log-async=20

* Query Control:
** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global:
*** Forward all queries to {rbrr_dns_server}
*** Return all responses unmodified

** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is allowlist:
*** Resolve-then-freeze: for each domain in {rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}, resolve A records via {rbrr_dns_server} at startup, then freeze as static `address=` entries in dnsmasq. No `server=` forwarding directives — dnsmasq synthesizes responses locally from frozen IPs. Subdomain queries (e.g., `exfil.anthropic.com`) return the frozen parent IP without generating upstream queries, closing the DNS subdomain exfiltration channel.
*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries (address=/#/)
--

//axhos_step
. Service Launch:
* Start dnsmasq daemon
* Wait for initialization (2 second delay)

//axhos_step
. Filter Rules:
+
--
* Accept DNS queries arriving at sentry from enclave:
** RBM-INGRESS: interface attached to {at_enclave_network}, UDP port 53
** RBM-INGRESS: interface attached to {at_enclave_network}, TCP port 53
* Accept sentry upstream DNS forwarding:
** RBM-EGRESS: interface attached to {at_transit_network}, UDP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
** RBM-EGRESS: interface attached to {at_transit_network}, TCP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
--

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Verify - about, vouch, and batch_vouch operations (director credentials)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFV_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfv_*)

zrbfv_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfv already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'Define vouch operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfv_vouch_"

  buc_log_args 'Define about operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFV_ABOUT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfv_about_"

  buc_log_args 'Define graft metadata operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfv_graft_meta_"

  readonly ZRBFV_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfv_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfv not kindled - call zrbfv_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbfv_*)

rbfv_vouch_gate() {
  zrbfv_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel="${1:-}"
  local -r z_hallmark="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "rbfv_vouch_gate: vessel required"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "rbfv_vouch_gate: hallmark required"

  # Vouch package = rbi_hm/<H>/vouch, tag = <H> (hallmark-as-tag).
  local -r z_vouch_tag="${z_hallmark}"
  buc_step "Vouch gate: checking ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_vouch_tag}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "rbfv_vouch_gate: failed to get Director OAuth token"

  local z_vouch_http_code
  curl --head -s \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
    -o /dev/null \
    -w "%{http_code}" \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}/manifests/${z_vouch_tag}" \
    > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "rbfv_vouch_gate: HEAD request failed for ${z_vessel}:${z_vouch_tag}"
  z_vouch_http_code=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")

  if test "${z_vouch_http_code}" != "200"; then
    buc_die "Hallmark not vouched: ${z_hallmark} (HTTP ${z_vouch_http_code} — refusing to use unvouched image)"
  fi

  buc_info "Vouch verified: ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_vouch_tag}"
}

rbfv_about() {
  zrbfv_sentinel

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_kludge}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, without authenticating to or fetching from Cloud Build or GAR. It produces a
5	{rbtga_hallmark}
6	with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark. Kludge resolves the same {rbrv_image_anchor} declarations that {rbtgo_ark_conjure} does — so an enshrined vessel built via kludge consumes the same GAR-anchored base layers as the conjure path — but anchored layers must be pre-cached locally; the credentialed fetch happens out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin-only slots (no anchor) require no credentials, so kludge pulls them inline if they're not already cached.
7	
8	NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.
9	
10	NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.
11	
12	The kludge operation:
13	
14	//axhopt_typed_parameter
15	{rbtga_ark}
16	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
17	.. {rbbc_require}
18	vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
19	.. {rbbc_require}
20	vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
21	.. {rbbc_require}
22	vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Resolve Base Images*:
26	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
27	{rbrv_image_origin}
28	is set:
29	... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is also set, resolve the slot's `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg to the full GAR reference composed from the locator (`«REGISTRY_HOST»/«PROJECT»/«REPOSITORY»/«PACKAGE_PATH»:«TAG»`) — same shape conjure produces
30	... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is empty, pass {rbrv_image_origin} through verbatim as the build-arg
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if a slot has {rbrv_image_anchor} set but {rbrv_image_origin} empty: "Malformed regime — anchor without origin"
33	
34	//axhos_step
35	. *Verify Local Image Presence*:
36	.. For each resolved slot ref, query the local container runtime (`docker image inspect`) to confirm the image is cached locally
37	.. On pass-through miss (origin without anchor): announce and run `docker pull «origin»` inline, then re-inspect to confirm the pull landed an image locally. No credentials are required for public upstream registries.
38	.. On anchored miss: accumulate the missed locator for post-loop refusal
39	.. {rbbc_fatal}
40	if any anchored miss accumulated, emit a remediation block pointing at {rbtgo_image_retrieve} for each missed locator and abort before invoking docker build
41	.. The credentialed fetch for anchored slots is deliberately out-of-band — kludge never authenticates against GAR. The inline origin pull reaches upstream without credentials and does not change this property.
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
45	.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	`«HALLMARK»`
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Build Image Locally*:
51	.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
52	.. The no-network property comes from the presence check above (every slot verified in the local cache — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent) combined with buildx's default behavior of not re-pulling base images. Hardcoded `FROM` stages that bypass `RBF_IMAGE_n` parameterization remain a residual risk; buildx no longer exposes a `--pull=never` analog, so explicit assertion of the no-network contract at build time is no longer available
53	.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
54	.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format
55	
56	//axhos_step
57	. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
58	.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree (`«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`)
59	.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
60	.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Report Hallmark*:
64	.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
65	.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining
66	
67	== Limitations
68	
69	* Requires GCP depot configuration to construct GAR-formatted image tags (registry host, project, repository), but no credentials are consumed and no registry is contacted for hallmark publication — only origin-slot upstream pulls reach a registry, unauthenticated
70	* Anchored base images must be cached locally before kludge runs — fetched out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin pass-through slots auto-pull inline if missing, so the cold-cache path for origin-only vessels is a single operator step; vessels with anchored slots remain two steps (wrest, then kludge).
71	* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
72	* No about artifact, no provenance chain -- the vouch alias carries no attestation content
73	* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks
74	
75	== Tabtarget
76	
77	`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`
78	
79	== See Also
80	
81	* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build; shares anchor-resolution semantics with kludge
82	* {rbtgo_image_retrieve} -- credentialed wrest of an enshrined base image into the local cache (remediation target when an anchored slot is missing)
83	* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
84	* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)
85

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_ark_kludge}

NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, without authenticating to or fetching from Cloud Build or GAR. It produces a
{rbtga_hallmark}
with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark. Kludge resolves the same {rbrv_image_anchor} declarations that {rbtgo_ark_conjure} does — so an enshrined vessel built via kludge consumes the same GAR-anchored base layers as the conjure path — but anchored layers must be pre-cached locally; the credentialed fetch happens out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin-only slots (no anchor) require no credentials, so kludge pulls them inline if they're not already cached.

NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.

NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.

The kludge operation:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtga_ark}
. *Validate Input Parameters*:
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)

//axhos_step
. *Resolve Base Images*:
.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
{rbrv_image_origin}
is set:
... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is also set, resolve the slot's `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg to the full GAR reference composed from the locator (`«REGISTRY_HOST»/«PROJECT»/«REPOSITORY»/«PACKAGE_PATH»:«TAG»`) — same shape conjure produces
... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is empty, pass {rbrv_image_origin} through verbatim as the build-arg
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if a slot has {rbrv_image_anchor} set but {rbrv_image_origin} empty: "Malformed regime — anchor without origin"

//axhos_step
. *Verify Local Image Presence*:
.. For each resolved slot ref, query the local container runtime (`docker image inspect`) to confirm the image is cached locally
.. On pass-through miss (origin without anchor): announce and run `docker pull «origin»` inline, then re-inspect to confirm the pull landed an image locally. No credentials are required for public upstream registries.
.. On anchored miss: accumulate the missed locator for post-loop refusal
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if any anchored miss accumulated, emit a remediation block pointing at {rbtgo_image_retrieve} for each missed locator and abort before invoking docker build
.. The credentialed fetch for anchored slots is deliberately out-of-band — kludge never authenticates against GAR. The inline origin pull reaches upstream without credentials and does not change this property.

//axhos_step
. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
.. {rbbc_store}
`«HALLMARK»`

//axhos_step
. *Build Image Locally*:
.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
.. The no-network property comes from the presence check above (every slot verified in the local cache — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent) combined with buildx's default behavior of not re-pulling base images. Hardcoded `FROM` stages that bypass `RBF_IMAGE_n` parameterization remain a residual risk; buildx no longer exposes a `--pull=never` analog, so explicit assertion of the no-network contract at build time is no longer available
.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format

//axhos_step
. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree (`«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`)
.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)

//axhos_step
. *Report Hallmark*:
.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining

== Limitations

* Requires GCP depot configuration to construct GAR-formatted image tags (registry host, project, repository), but no credentials are consumed and no registry is contacted for hallmark publication — only origin-slot upstream pulls reach a registry, unauthenticated
* Anchored base images must be cached locally before kludge runs — fetched out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin pass-through slots auto-pull inline if missing, so the cold-cache path for origin-only vessels is a single operator step; vessels with anchored slots remain two steps (wrest, then kludge).
* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
* No about artifact, no provenance chain -- the vouch alias carries no attestation content
* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks

== Tabtarget

`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`

== See Also

* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build; shares anchor-resolution semantics with kludge
* {rbtgo_image_retrieve} -- credentialed wrest of an enshrined base image into the local cache (remediation target when an anchored slot is missing)
* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)

file.content:
Propagation retry per <<rbscip_trade_study>> backoff profile (3s initial, 2x multiplier, 20s cap, 420s deadline).
API contract details: <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.

== Script Internals

Detailed implementation reference for security enforcement internals within the
{at_sentry_container}.

[[scr_security_config]]
//axvo_procedure axd_transient
=== {scr_security_config}

Establishes security controls through four phases executed in sequence, halting early if any command has a nonzero unix status.

include::RBSSC-security_config.adoc[]

[[scr_iptables_init]]
//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal
==== {scr_iptables_init}

Establishes the basic IPTables rules and chains within a
{at_sentry_container}.

include::RBSII-iptables_init.adoc[]

[[scr_port_setup]]
//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal
==== {scr_port_setup}

Establishes port mapping and service exposure rules within a
{at_sentry_container}.

include::RBSPT-port_setup.adoc[]

[[scr_access_setup]]
//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal
==== {scr_access_setup}

Configures network access controls including NAT rules and connection tracking.

include::RBSAX-access_setup.adoc[]

[[scr_dns_step]]
//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal
==== {scr_dns_step}

Configures DNS resolution controls and domain access restrictions.

include::RBSDS-dns_step.adoc[]

== Trade Studies

Trade studies document the crystallized results of intensive research into complex technical domains.
Each study presents constraints, evaluated alternatives, and the rationale for chosen solutions—without the discovery narrative.

[[rbsob_oci_layout_bridge]]
=== OCI Layout Bridge

include::RBSOB-oci_layout_bridge.adoc[]

content.content:
1270	[[rbtgo_ark_conjure]]
1271	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1272	=== {rbtgo_ark_conjure}
1273	
1274	Conjures an
1275	{rbtga_ark_image}
1276	by building a container image
1277	({rbtgog_ark})
1278	from a vessel specification.
1279	Build context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}, submitted via `builds.create` API. The combined job produces both the `image` and `about` packages under the hallmark subtree (about steps embedded via stitch). Vouch is a separate Cloud Build job using DSSE envelope signature verification.
1280	
1281	include::RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc[]
1282	
1283	[[rbtgo_ark_enshrine]]
1284	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1285	=== {rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
1286	
1287	Enshrines upstream base images into
1288	{gar_service}
1289	({rbtgog_ark}),
1290	producing content-addressed
1291	{rbrv_image_anchor}
1292	references from
1293	{rbrv_image_origin}
1294	declarations.
1295	The
1296	{rbtr_director}
1297	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each upstream image by tag to GAR with a human-readable anchor tag, and writes the anchor back to the vessel regime.
1298	
1299	include::RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc[]
1300	
1301	[[rbtgo_ark_graft]]
1302	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1303	=== {rbtgo_ark_graft}
1304	
1305	Grafts a locally-built container image into the
1306	{rbtgi_repository}
1307	({rbtgog_ark})
1308	via native docker/podman push.
1309	The image push itself is local (no Cloud Build); about and vouch follow as Cloud Build jobs.
1310	
1311	include::RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc[]
1312	
1313	[[rbtgo_ark_kludge]]
1314	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1315	=== {rbtgo_ark_kludge}
1316	
1317	Kludges a locally-built container image for development iteration
1318	({rbtgog_ark}).
1319	No registry push, no Cloud Build, no credentials — `docker build` runs against the workstation cache and produces a `k`-prefixed
1320	{rbtga_hallmark}
1321	that composes cleanly with nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark.
1322	Kludge resolves
1323	{rbrv_image_anchor}
1324	declarations to GAR references identically to {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, but anchored images must already be locally cached; the credentialed fetch happens out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin-only slots auto-pull inline — no credentials required for public upstream.
1325	
1326	include::RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc[]
1327	
1328	[[rbtgo_ark_about]]
1329	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1330	=== {rbtgo_ark_about}
1331	
1332	Assembles the
1333	{rbtga_ark_about}
1334	metadata artifact for an existing
1335	{rbtga_ark_image}
1336	({rbtgog_ark}).
1337	Runs Syft for SBOM generation and builds mode-aware `build_info.json` using uniform
1338	{gcb_service}
1339	steps.
