1	FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm
2	
3	# Define ARG and ENV: defaults for building remotely, from nameplate when used locally
4	ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=8000
5	ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}
6	
7	ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=/workspace
8	ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}
9	
10	# Add Jupyter-specific environment variables
11	ENV JUPYTER_BASE_URL="/"
12	ENV JUPYTER_IP="0.0.0.0"
13	
14	# Install system dependencies and diagnostic tools
15	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y                                       \
16	    iputils-ping         `#For network connectivity tests`                     \
17	    iproute2             `#For network configuration`                          \
18	    net-tools            `#For network interface configuration and statistics` \
19	    dnsutils             `#For DNS lookups and diagnostics`                    \
20	    curl                 `#For making HTTP requests`                           \
21	    traceroute           `#For tracing network routes`                         \
22	    tcpdump              `#For packet analysis`                                \
23	    nmap                 `#For network discovery`                              \
24	    procps               `#For ps, top, etc.`                                  \
25	    netcat-openbsd       `#For network testing`                                \
26	    lsof                 `#For checking open ports/files`                      \
27	    htop                 `#For process monitoring`                             \
28	    strace               `#For system call tracing`                            \
29	    vim                  `#For file editing`                                   \
30	    wget                 `#For downloading files`                              \
31	    telnet               `#For testing TCP connections`                        \
32	    conntrack            `#For connection tracking`                            \
33	    socat                `#For multipurpose relay`                             \
34	    iptraf-ng            `#For interactive network monitoring`                 \
35	    iftop                `#For bandwidth monitoring`                           \
36	    mtr                  `#For network diagnostic combining ping & traceroute` \
37	    ethtool              `#For network interface card settings`                \
38	    bridge-utils         `#For bridge network diagnostics`                     \
39	    psmisc               `#For fuser, killall, etc.`                           \
40	    ipcalc               `#For IP calculations and subnet info`                \
41	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
42	
43	RUN pip install --no-cache-dir jupyter jupyterlab
44	RUN pip install --no-cache-dir anthropic
45	
46	# Set the working directory to the mount point
47	WORKDIR ${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}
48	
49	# Generate Jupyter config and set dark theme using settings override
50	RUN jupyter lab --generate-config                                                                                                         &&\
51	    mkdir -p                                 /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension                              &&\
52	    echo '{"theme": "JupyterLab Dark"}'   >  /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension/themes.jupyterlab-settings   &&\
53	    echo "c.ServerApp.allow_origin = '*'" >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py                                                       &&\
54	    echo "c.ServerApp.ip = '0.0.0.0'"     >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py
55	
56	# Create a startup script with enhanced logging
57	RUN echo '#!/bin/sh'                                                                     >  /startup.sh  &&\
58	    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter environment with enhanced logging..."'                 >>  /startup.sh  &&\
59	    echo 'echo "Network interface configuration:"'                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
60	    echo 'ip addr'                                                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
61	    echo 'echo "Current listening ports:"'                                              >>  /startup.sh  &&\
62	    echo 'netstat -tuln'                                                                >>  /startup.sh  &&\
63	    echo 'echo "Disable nag: Would you like to receive official Jupyter news?"'         >>  /startup.sh  &&\
64	    echo 'jupyter labextension disable "@jupyterlab/apputils-extension:announcements"'  >>  /startup.sh  &&\
65	    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter Lab server..."'                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
66	    echo 'jupyter lab'                                        \
67	         '--ip=0.0.0.0'                                       \
68	         '--port=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}'                 \
69	         '--no-browser'                                       \
70	         '--allow-root'                                       \
71	         '--ServerApp.token=""'                               \
72	         '--ServerApp.password=""'                            \
73	         '--ServerApp.authenticate_prometheus=False'          \
74	         '--ServerApp.base_url=${JUPYTER_BASE_URL}'           \
75	         '--notebook-dir=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}'              \
76	         '--ServerApp.allow_origin="*"'                       \
77	         '--log-level=DEBUG'                                  \
78	                                                                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
79	    echo 'tail -f /dev/null'                                                            >>  /startup.sh  &&\
80	    chmod +x                                                                                /startup.sh
81	
82	# Expose the Jupyter port
83	EXPOSE ${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}
84	
85	# Use the startup script as the entry point
86	CMD ["/startup.sh"]
87

file.content:
FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm

# Define ARG and ENV: defaults for building remotely, from nameplate when used locally
ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=8000
ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}

ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=/workspace
ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}

# Add Jupyter-specific environment variables
ENV JUPYTER_BASE_URL="/"
ENV JUPYTER_IP="0.0.0.0"

# Install system dependencies and diagnostic tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y                                       \
    iputils-ping         `#For network connectivity tests`                     \
    iproute2             `#For network configuration`                          \
    net-tools            `#For network interface configuration and statistics` \
    dnsutils             `#For DNS lookups and diagnostics`                    \
    curl                 `#For making HTTP requests`                           \
    traceroute           `#For tracing network routes`                         \
    tcpdump              `#For packet analysis`                                \
    nmap                 `#For network discovery`                              \
    procps               `#For ps, top, etc.`                                  \
    netcat-openbsd       `#For network testing`                                \
    lsof                 `#For checking open ports/files`                      \
    htop                 `#For process monitoring`                             \
    strace               `#For system call tracing`                            \
    vim                  `#For file editing`                                   \
    wget                 `#For downloading files`                              \
    telnet               `#For testing TCP connections`                        \
    conntrack            `#For connection tracking`                            \
    socat                `#For multipurpose relay`                             \
    iptraf-ng            `#For interactive network monitoring`                 \
    iftop                `#For bandwidth monitoring`                           \
    mtr                  `#For network diagnostic combining ping & traceroute` \
    ethtool              `#For network interface card settings`                \
    bridge-utils         `#For bridge network diagnostics`                     \
    psmisc               `#For fuser, killall, etc.`                           \
    ipcalc               `#For IP calculations and subnet info`                \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir jupyter jupyterlab
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir anthropic

# Set the working directory to the mount point
WORKDIR ${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}

# Generate Jupyter config and set dark theme using settings override
RUN jupyter lab --generate-config                                                                                                         &&\
    mkdir -p                                 /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension                              &&\
    echo '{"theme": "JupyterLab Dark"}'   >  /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension/themes.jupyterlab-settings   &&\
    echo "c.ServerApp.allow_origin = '*'" >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py                                                       &&\
    echo "c.ServerApp.ip = '0.0.0.0'"     >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py

# Create a startup script with enhanced logging
RUN echo '#!/bin/sh'                                                                     >  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter environment with enhanced logging..."'                 >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Network interface configuration:"'                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'ip addr'                                                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Current listening ports:"'                                              >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'netstat -tuln'                                                                >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Disable nag: Would you like to receive official Jupyter news?"'         >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'jupyter labextension disable "@jupyterlab/apputils-extension:announcements"'  >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter Lab server..."'                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'jupyter lab'                                        \
         '--ip=0.0.0.0'                                       \
         '--port=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}'                 \
         '--no-browser'                                       \
         '--allow-root'                                       \
         '--ServerApp.token=""'                               \
         '--ServerApp.password=""'                            \
         '--ServerApp.authenticate_prometheus=False'          \
         '--ServerApp.base_url=${JUPYTER_BASE_URL}'           \
         '--notebook-dir=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}'              \
         '--ServerApp.allow_origin="*"'                       \
         '--log-level=DEBUG'                                  \
                                                                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'tail -f /dev/null'                                                            >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    chmod +x                                                                                /startup.sh

# Expose the Jupyter port
EXPOSE ${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}

# Use the startup script as the entry point
CMD ["/startup.sh"]

content.subject: Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene module + spec subdoc
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI, public rbfh_dockerfile_check, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel) and RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc (RBSDS shape, MCM-annotated, mint quoin rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene)

content.subject: Wire callsites and CLI furnishings
content.description: Source rbfh in rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh + zrbfh_kindle calls; call rbfh_dockerfile_check in rbfk_kludge (before docker build) and rbfd_build (after test -f validation)

content.subject: Fold BCG cleanups into rbfk_kludge.sh
content.description: Split multi-var locals, (( ${#arr[@]} )) over test -gt 0, extract zrbfk_image_present_predicate consolidating the three docker image inspect sites with counter-discriminated forensic stderr (ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX kindle constant + z_rbfk_inspect_counter mutable kindle state)

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Bottle ccyolo (Conjuring)

# Core Identity
RBRV_SIGIL=rbev-bottle-ccyolo
RBRV_DESCRIPTION="Claude Code sandbox with SSH entry for network-contained AI development"
RBRV_USER=claude

# Vessel Mode
RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=conjure

# Egress Mode
RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=tether

# Tool Image Reliquary
RBRV_RELIQUARY=r260513125123

# Conjuring Configuration
RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRV_SIGIL}/build-context/Dockerfile"
RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRV_SIGIL}/build-context"
RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64 linux/arm64"

# Base Images (ARG/FROM pattern — resolved by Foundry at conjure time)
RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=node:22-slim

# eof

# Ifrit forge — pre-stages apt packages and warms cargo dependency cache
#
# Build-time fixture, not a runtime vessel. The forge image is conjured
# tethered, then enshrined into GAR as a base for the airgap ifrit build.
# By pre-staging all apt packages and compiled Rust dependencies, the
# airgap build needs zero network access — only project source compiles.

# Build context: rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-forge-context/
# Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock must stay in sync with common-ifrit-context/.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation conjures an
5	{rbtga_ark_image}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by building a container image from a vessel specification. The
9	{rbtr_director}
10	performs the build via
11	{gcb_service}
12	using direct `builds.create` API submission.
13	
14	NOTE: Conjure produces both the image and about artifacts in a single Cloud Build job submitted via `builds.create`.
15	The about steps (`rbgja01`-`rbgja04`) are embedded via stitch alongside the build steps.
16	The vouch artifact is created by the subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline stage (always a separate Cloud Build job).
17	
18	NOTE: The
19	{rbtga_hallmark}
20	is minted locally by the
21	{rbtr_director}
22	before submission.
23	The Director knows the complete artifact tag set before the build begins -- naming authority and verification authority are the same entity.
24	
25	NOTE: The conjure build job runs on the worker pool selected by the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`: tether pool for vessels needing public internet (e.g., `apt-get` in Dockerfile), airgap pool for vessels with all dependencies enshrined in GAR.
26	The subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} always runs on the airgap pool regardless of vessel egress mode.
27	
28	NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.
29	
30	NOTE: The conjure Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the extract-context step. Conjure populates `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n` substitutions from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` slots, so the preflight HEAD-checks each anchored base in addition to the reliquary tools.
31	
32	The conjure operation:
33	
34	//axhopt_typed_parameter
35	{rbtga_ark}
36	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
37	.. {rbbc_require}
38	vessel provided as first argument
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	vessel contains conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Registry Preflight*:
44	.. *Reliquary check*: HEAD request to `«RELIQUARY»/docker:latest` manifest in
45	{gar_service}.
46	{rbbc_require}
47	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run inscribe first.
48	The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images inscribed from upstream into private GAR. Each vessel declares its reliquary; multiple datestamped reliquaries can coexist.
49	.. *Enshrine check*: For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where both
50	{rbrv_image_origin}
51	and
52	{rbrv_image_anchor}
53	are set:
54	... Parse
55	{rbrv_image_anchor}
56	as a
57	{rbst_locator}
58	into package-path and tag
59	... HEAD request to `«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»` manifest in
60	{gar_service}
61	... {rbbc_require}
62	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} first.
63	Multiple vessels sharing the same anchor need only one enshrine.
64	.. Preflight prevents wasted
65	{gcb_service}
66	submission time on missing images, with copy-paste remediation commands
67	
68	//axhos_step
69	. *Resolve Base Images*:
70	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
71	{rbrv_image_origin}
72	is set:
73	... If
74	{rbrv_image_anchor}
75	is present: parse the
76	{rbst_locator}
77	and resolve to full GAR image reference (`«GAR-REPO-BASE»/«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»`)
78	... If
79	{rbrv_image_anchor}
80	is absent: pass
81	{rbrv_image_origin}
82	through as the upstream tag (development path)
83	.. Resolved references become `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args for the Dockerfile `ARG`/`FROM` pattern
84	
85	//axhos_step
86	. *Assign Hallmark*:
87	.. Mint
88	{rbtga_hallmark}
89	locally on the Director's workstation
90	.. {rbbc_store}
91	`«HALLMARK»` -- complete artifact tag set is now known before build submission
92	
93	//axhos_step
94	. *Push Pouch*:
95	.. Package build context (Dockerfile and context files) as a FROM SCRATCH OCI image
96	.. Push to GAR tagged `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/pouch:«HALLMARK»`
97	.. {rbbc_require}
98	push succeeds -- the pouch delivers build context to
99	{gcb_service}
100	
101	//axhos_step
102	. *Execute Build*:
103	.. Stitch
104	{rbtgr_build_json}
105	with all step image references and substitution variables
106	.. {rbbc_submit}
107	`POST «GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL»` with Build resource JSON via `builds.create`
108	.. All build parameters in stitched
109	{rbtgr_build_json}
110	-- zero custom substitution overrides
111	.. Waits for build completion
112	.. On success, the
113	{rbtga_ark_image}
114	artifact is created with the
115	{rbtga_hallmark}
116	.. {rbtgr_provenance}
117	is auto-generated by Google as a signed attestation on the built image
118	
119	//axhoot_typed_output
120	{rbtga_ark_image}
121	
122	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
123	{rbf_fact_hallmark}
124	The full
125	{rbtga_hallmark}
126	string.
127	
128	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
129	{rbf_fact_build_id}
130	The
131	{gcb_build}
132	job ID from the `builds.create` submission.
133	Used by
134	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
135	for
136	{rbtgr_provenance}
137	verification.
138	
139	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
140	{rbf_fact_gar_root}
141	{gar_service}
142	registry prefix.
143	Composable with
144	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
145	for full artifact references.
146	
147	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
148	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
149	Hallmark subtree prefix joining
150	{gar_service}
151	root with the hallmark path segment as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»`.
152	Composable with basename and `:«HALLMARK»` tag for full artifact references.
153	
154	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
155	{rbf_fact_ark_yield}
156	Per-platform
157	{rbtga_ark_image}
158	reference.
159	One fact file per platform discovered during the build.
160	
161	. *Display Results*:
162	.. {rbbc_show}
163	vessel image built with hallmark tag
164	
165	//axhoc_completion
166	Successful execution creates the
167	{rbtga_ark_image}
168	in
169	{rbtgi_repository}.
170	The {rbtr_director} retains `«BUILD_ID»` and `«HALLMARK»` from the conjure round-trip.
171	The `builds.create` job produces both the image and about artifacts under the hallmark subtree (about steps embedded via stitch).
172	{rbtgo_ark_ordain} chains only {rbtgo_ark_vouch} after the combined job completes, passing `«BUILD_ID»` for DSSE provenance verification.
173

file.content:
rbfd_build() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Build container image from vessel via direct builds.create submission"
  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_log_args "Validate parameters"
  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_sigils
    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
    buc_step "Available vessels:"
    local z_sigil=""
    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
    done
    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
  fi

  # Load and validate vessel
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  buc_log_args "Verify vessel has conjuring configuration"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not configured for conjuring (no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE)"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not configured for conjuring (no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)"

  buc_log_args "Resolve paths from vessel configuration"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"

  buc_info "Building vessel image: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (required for step image references)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Source Director RBRA for credentials
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Quota preflight -- warn if insufficient capacity
  zrbfd_quota_preflight "${z_token}"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfd_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfd_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfd_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Director Build" zrbfd_furnish "$@"

# eof

content.subject: Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene module + spec subdoc
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI, public rbfh_dockerfile_check, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel) and RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc (RBSDS shape, MCM-annotated, mint quoin rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene)

content.subject: Wire callsites and CLI furnishings
content.description: Source rbfh in rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh + zrbfh_kindle calls; call rbfh_dockerfile_check in rbfk_kludge (before docker build) and rbfd_build (after test -f validation)

content.subject: Fold BCG cleanups into rbfk_kludge.sh
content.description: Split multi-var locals, (( ${#arr[@]} )) over test -gt 0, extract zrbfk_image_present_predicate consolidating the three docker image inspect sites with counter-discriminated forensic stderr (ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX kindle constant + z_rbfk_inspect_counter mutable kindle state)

content.subject: Remediate rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel
content.description: Verify RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN field shape against working conjure vessel; rewrite jupyter Dockerfile from hardcoded FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm to ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}; update vessel rbrv.env

content.subject: Spec cross-refs and CLAUDE.md mappings
content.description: RBSAK Step 6 reference to {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}, RBSAC reference at build dispatch, CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings add RBFH and RBSFH entries

content.subject: Verification: fast suite + pristine kludge sentry
content.description: bash -n on all touched modules; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green; tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor completes through hallmark publication; jupyter vessel passes hygiene post-remediation

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene - Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfh_*)

zrbfh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfh already kindled"
  readonly ZRBFH_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBFH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfh not kindled - call zrbfh_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfh_*)

# Dockerfile Hygiene Check
#
# Enforces project-wide constraints on Dockerfile FROM lines so that the
# ARG/${RBF_IMAGE_n} parameterization stays the sole source of base
# image origin. Hygiene violations short-circuit before any build, in
# both the local kludge and the Cloud Build conjure paths.
#
# Rules apply to every line at column 0 starting with FROM followed by
# whitespace, after column-0 `#` comments are filtered out. Violation
# emits {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision via buc_die.
# Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible. Empty
# Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently.
rbfh_dockerfile_check() {
  zrbfh_sentinel

  local -r z_path="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile path required"
  test -f "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_line=""
  local z_lineno=0
  local z_token2=""
  local z_pre=""
  local z_post=""

  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lineno=$(( z_lineno + 1 ))

    # Docker treats only column-0 `#` as a comment
    case "${z_line}" in
      '#'*) continue ;;
    esac

    # Constrain only column-0 FROM lines followed by whitespace
    [[ "${z_line}" =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]] || continue

    # Rule 1: no tab character anywhere on the FROM line
    case "${z_line}" in
      *$'\t'*) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line contains tab character — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac

    # Rule 3: line does not end with `\`
    case "${z_line}" in
      *'\') buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line ends with backslash continuation — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac

    # Rule 2: second whitespace-delimited token must be approved
    read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}"
    case "${z_token2}" in
      '${RBF_IMAGE_1}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_2}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_3}'|'scratch') : ;;
      *) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM image token must be \${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_path}"
}

# eof

# ARG/${RBF_IMAGE_n} parameterization stays the sole source of base
# image origin. Hygiene violations short-circuit before any build, in
# both the local kludge and the Cloud Build conjure paths.
#
# Rules apply to every line at column 0 starting with FROM followed by
# whitespace, after column-0 `#` comments are filtered out. Violation
# emits {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision via buc_die.
# Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible. Empty
# Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently.
rbfh_dockerfile_check() {
  zrbfh_sentinel

  local -r z_path="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile path required"
  test -f "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_line=""
  local z_lineno=0
  local z_token2=""
  local z_pre=""
  local z_post=""

  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lineno=$(( z_lineno + 1 ))

    # Docker treats only column-0 `#` as a comment
    case "${z_line}" in
      '#'*) continue ;;
    esac

    # Constrain only column-0 FROM lines followed by whitespace
    [[ "${z_line}" =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]] || continue

    # Rule 1: no tab character anywhere on the FROM line
    case "${z_line}" in
      *$'\t'*) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line contains tab character — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac

    # Rule 3: line does not end with `\`
    case "${z_line}" in
      *'\') buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line ends with backslash continuation — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac

    # Rule 2: second whitespace-delimited token must be approved
    read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}"
    case "${z_token2}" in
      '${RBF_IMAGE_1}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_2}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_3}'|'scratch') : ;;
      *) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM image token must be \${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch — ${z_line}" ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_path}"
}

#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfk_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfk_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfk_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge" zrbfk_furnish "$@"

# Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge - local image build for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR, no credentials)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFK_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFK_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)

zrbfk_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfk_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfk_*)

######################################################################
# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
#
# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
#
# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

rbfk_kludge() {
  zrbfk_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Load vessel configuration
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"

  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_build_args=()
  local z_slot="" z_origin_var="" z_anchor_var="" z_origin="" z_anchor=""
  local z_slot_ref="" z_pkg_path="" z_tag="" z_miss=""
  local z_anchored_misses=()
  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
    fi
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
      fi
    else
      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
      fi
    fi

    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
  done
  test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0 || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

  if test "${#z_anchored_misses[@]}" -gt 0; then
    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
    buc_bare ""
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
    done
    buc_bare ""
    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
  fi

  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
  local -r z_hallmark="${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"

  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"

  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
  buc_step "Building image locally"
  docker build \
    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"

  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
}

# eof

input.new_string:
zrbfk_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Define forensic temp prefix for docker image inspect stderr capture'
  readonly ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_"

  buc_log_args 'Initialize mutable image-presence inspect counter'
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0

  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfk_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
}

# Image-Presence Predicate
#
# Wraps `docker image inspect` so stderr lands in a counter-discriminated
# forensic temp file rather than the terminal. Returns docker's exit
# status; callers consume via `if zrbfk_image_present_predicate ...` or
# `... || ...`. The forensic files persist in BURD_TEMP_DIR for the
# command's lifetime, available for post-mortem inspection.
zrbfk_image_present_predicate() {
  zrbfk_sentinel
  local -r z_ref="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_ref}" || buc_die "zrbfk_image_present_predicate: image ref required"
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=$(( z_rbfk_inspect_counter + 1 ))
  docker image inspect "${z_ref}" \
    >/dev/null \
    2>"${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${z_rbfk_inspect_counter}"
}

  local z_build_args=()

  local z_slot_ref="" z_pkg_path="" z_tag="" z_miss=""
  local z_anchored_misses=()
  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
    fi
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
      fi
    else
      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker image inspect "${z_slot_ref}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
      fi
    fi

    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
  done
  test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0 || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

  if test "${#z_anchored_misses[@]}" -gt 0; then

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge - local image build for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR, no credentials)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBFK_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBFK_SOURCED=1
26	
27	# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
28	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)
32	
33	zrbfk_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"
35	
36	  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
37	  zrbfc_kindle
38	
39	  buc_log_args 'Define forensic temp prefix for docker image inspect stderr capture'
40	  readonly ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_"
41	
42	  buc_log_args 'Initialize mutable image-presence inspect counter'
43	  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0
44	
45	  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
46	}
47	
48	zrbfk_sentinel() {
49	  zrbfc_sentinel
50	  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
51	}
52	
53	# Image-Presence Predicate
54	#
55	# Wraps `docker image inspect` so stderr lands in a counter-discriminated
56	# forensic temp file rather than the terminal. Returns docker's exit
57	# status; callers consume via `if zrbfk_image_present_predicate ...` or
58	# `... || ...`. The forensic files persist in BURD_TEMP_DIR for the
59	# command's lifetime, available for post-mortem inspection.
60	zrbfk_image_present_predicate() {
61	  zrbfk_sentinel
62	  local -r z_ref="${1:-}"
63	  test -n "${z_ref}" || buc_die "zrbfk_image_present_predicate: image ref required"
64	  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=$(( z_rbfk_inspect_counter + 1 ))
65	  docker image inspect "${z_ref}" \
66	    >/dev/null \
67	    2>"${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${z_rbfk_inspect_counter}"
68	}
69	
70	######################################################################
71	# External Functions (rbfk_*)
72	
73	######################################################################
74	# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
75	#
76	# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
77	# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
78	# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
79	# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
80	#
81	# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
82	# Host platform only (no multi-arch).
83	
84	rbfk_kludge() {
85	  zrbfk_sentinel
86	
87	  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
88	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
89	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
90	
91	  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
92	  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
93	  git diff --quiet \
94	    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
95	  git diff --cached --quiet \
96	    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"
97	
98	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
99	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
100	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
101	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
102	
103	  # Load vessel configuration
104	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
105	
106	  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
107	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
108	    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
109	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
110	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
111	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
112	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
113	  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
114	    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
115	  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
116	    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"
117	
118	  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
119	  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
120	  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
121	  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
122	  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
123	  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
124	  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
125	  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
126	  local z_build_args=()
127	  local z_slot=""
128	  local z_origin_var=""
129	  local z_anchor_var=""
130	  local z_origin=""
131	  local z_anchor=""
132	  local z_slot_ref=""
133	  local z_pkg_path=""
134	  local z_tag=""
135	  local z_miss=""
136	  local z_anchored_misses=()
137	  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
138	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
139	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
140	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
141	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
142	
143	    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
144	      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
145	    fi
146	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
147	
148	    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
149	      case "${z_anchor}" in
150	        *:*) : ;;
151	        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
152	      esac
153	      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
154	      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
155	      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
156	      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
157	      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
158	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
159	      zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
160	        || z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
161	    else
162	      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
163	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
164	      if ! zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}"; then
165	        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
166	        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
167	          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
168	        zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
169	          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
170	      fi
171	    fi
172	
173	    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
174	  done
175	  (( ${#z_build_args[@]} )) || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"
176	
177	  if (( ${#z_anchored_misses[@]} )); then
178	    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
179	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
180	    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
181	    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
182	    buc_bare ""
183	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
184	    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
185	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
186	    done
187	    buc_bare ""
188	    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
189	  fi
190	
191	  buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene"
192	  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
193	
194	  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
195	  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
196	  local -r z_hallmark="${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"
197	
198	  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
199	  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
200	  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
201	
202	  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
203	  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
204	  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"
205	
206	  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
207	  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
208	  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
209	  buc_step "Building image locally"
210	  docker build \
211	    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
212	    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
213	    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
214	    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
215	    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
216	
217	  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
218	  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
219	  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
220	    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"
221	
222	  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
223	  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
224	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
225	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
226	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"
227	
228	  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
229	  buc_bare ""
230	  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
231	  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
232	  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
233	  buc_bare ""
234	  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
235	}
236	
237	# eof
238

#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Kludge - local image build for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR, no credentials)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFK_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFK_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module (registry constants + vessel resolution helpers)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfk_*)

zrbfk_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfk already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Define forensic temp prefix for docker image inspect stderr capture'
  readonly ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_"

  buc_log_args 'Initialize mutable image-presence inspect counter'
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0

  readonly ZRBFK_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfk_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFK_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfk not kindled - call zrbfk_kindle first"
}

# Image-Presence Predicate
#
# Wraps `docker image inspect` so stderr lands in a counter-discriminated
# forensic temp file rather than the terminal. Returns docker's exit
# status; callers consume via `if zrbfk_image_present_predicate ...` or
# `... || ...`. The forensic files persist in BURD_TEMP_DIR for the
# command's lifetime, available for post-mortem inspection.
zrbfk_image_present_predicate() {
  zrbfk_sentinel
  local -r z_ref="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_ref}" || buc_die "zrbfk_image_present_predicate: image ref required"
  z_rbfk_inspect_counter=$(( z_rbfk_inspect_counter + 1 ))
  docker image inspect "${z_ref}" \
    >/dev/null \
    2>"${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${z_rbfk_inspect_counter}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfk_*)

######################################################################
# Kludge Build - Local image build for development
#
# Builds a vessel image locally using docker build, tags it with a
# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style
# format that compose and rbob_charge expect. Also creates a fake
# vouch tag (same image, aliased) so the vouch gate passes.
#
# No Cloud Build, no GAR push, no credentials consumed.
# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

rbfk_kludge() {
  zrbfk_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Load vessel configuration
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
  test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Build context not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}"

  # Resolve base images — mirror conjure's anchor-aware resolution so an enshrined
  # vessel built via kludge resolves the same GAR-anchored layers as conjure.
  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_build_args=()
  local z_slot=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_slot_ref=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_miss=""
  local z_anchored_misses=()
  for z_slot in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_slot}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    if test -z "${z_origin}" && test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      buc_die "Malformed regime: ${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor} set but ${z_origin_var} is empty"
    fi
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (anchored): ${z_slot_ref}"
      zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
        || z_anchored_misses+=("${z_anchor}")
    else
      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"
      if ! zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}"; then
        buc_info "Origin image not cached — pulling from upstream: ${z_slot_ref}"
        docker pull "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
        zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"
      fi
    fi

    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
  done
  (( ${#z_build_args[@]} )) || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"

  if (( ${#z_anchored_misses[@]} )); then
    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."
    buc_bare ""
    buc_bare "  Anchored slots (wrest from GAR):"
    for z_miss in "${z_anchored_misses[@]}"; do
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE}" "${z_miss}"
    done
    buc_bare ""
    buc_die "Local image cache incomplete — see remediation above"
  fi

  buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene"
  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"

  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
  local -r z_hallmark="${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"

  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"

  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
  buc_step "Building image locally"
  docker build \
    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"

  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
}

# eof

FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm

# Define ARG and ENV: defaults for building remotely, from nameplate when used locally
toolUseResult.newString:
# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)
ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

# Define ARG and ENV: defaults for building remotely, from nameplate when used locally
toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm

# Define ARG and ENV: defaults for building remotely, from nameplate when used locally
ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=8000
ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}

ARG RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=/workspace
ENV RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}

# Add Jupyter-specific environment variables
ENV JUPYTER_BASE_URL="/"
ENV JUPYTER_IP="0.0.0.0"

# Install system dependencies and diagnostic tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y                                       \
    iputils-ping         `#For network connectivity tests`                     \
    iproute2             `#For network configuration`                          \
    net-tools            `#For network interface configuration and statistics` \
    dnsutils             `#For DNS lookups and diagnostics`                    \
    curl                 `#For making HTTP requests`                           \
    traceroute           `#For tracing network routes`                         \
    tcpdump              `#For packet analysis`                                \
    nmap                 `#For network discovery`                              \
    procps               `#For ps, top, etc.`                                  \
    netcat-openbsd       `#For network testing`                                \
    lsof                 `#For checking open ports/files`                      \
    htop                 `#For process monitoring`                             \
    strace               `#For system call tracing`                            \
    vim                  `#For file editing`                                   \
    wget                 `#For downloading files`                              \
    telnet               `#For testing TCP connections`                        \
    conntrack            `#For connection tracking`                            \
    socat                `#For multipurpose relay`                             \
    iptraf-ng            `#For interactive network monitoring`                 \
    iftop                `#For bandwidth monitoring`                           \
    mtr                  `#For network diagnostic combining ping & traceroute` \
    ethtool              `#For network interface card settings`                \
    bridge-utils         `#For bridge network diagnostics`                     \
    psmisc               `#For fuser, killall, etc.`                           \
    ipcalc               `#For IP calculations and subnet info`                \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir jupyter jupyterlab
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir anthropic

# Set the working directory to the mount point
WORKDIR ${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}

# Generate Jupyter config and set dark theme using settings override
RUN jupyter lab --generate-config                                                                                                         &&\
    mkdir -p                                 /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension                              &&\
    echo '{"theme": "JupyterLab Dark"}'   >  /root/.jupyter/lab/user-settings/@jupyterlab/apputils-extension/themes.jupyterlab-settings   &&\
    echo "c.ServerApp.allow_origin = '*'" >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py                                                       &&\
    echo "c.ServerApp.ip = '0.0.0.0'"     >> /root/.jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py

# Create a startup script with enhanced logging
RUN echo '#!/bin/sh'                                                                     >  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter environment with enhanced logging..."'                 >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Network interface configuration:"'                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'ip addr'                                                                      >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Current listening ports:"'                                              >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'netstat -tuln'                                                                >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Disable nag: Would you like to receive official Jupyter news?"'         >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'jupyter labextension disable "@jupyterlab/apputils-extension:announcements"'  >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'echo "Starting Jupyter Lab server..."'                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'jupyter lab'                                        \
         '--ip=0.0.0.0'                                       \
         '--port=${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}'                 \
         '--no-browser'                                       \
         '--allow-root'                                       \
         '--ServerApp.token=""'                               \
         '--ServerApp.password=""'                            \
         '--ServerApp.authenticate_prometheus=False'          \
         '--ServerApp.base_url=${JUPYTER_BASE_URL}'           \
         '--notebook-dir=${RBEV_BOTTLE_WORKDIR}'              \
         '--ServerApp.allow_origin="*"'                       \
         '--log-level=DEBUG'                                  \
                                                                                        >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    echo 'tail -f /dev/null'                                                            >>  /startup.sh  &&\
    chmod +x                                                                                /startup.sh

# Expose the Jupyter port
EXPOSE ${RBEV_BOTTLE_JUPYTER_PORT}

# Use the startup script as the entry point
CMD ["/startup.sh"]

//axhos_step
. *Build Image Locally*:
.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
.. The no-network property comes from the presence check above (every slot verified in the local cache — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent) combined with buildx's default behavior of not re-pulling base images. Hardcoded `FROM` stages that bypass `RBF_IMAGE_n` parameterization remain a residual risk; buildx no longer exposes a `--pull=never` analog, so explicit assertion of the no-network contract at build time is no longer available
.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_ark_kludge}

NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, without authenticating to or fetching from Cloud Build or GAR. It produces a
{rbtga_hallmark}
with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark. Kludge resolves the same {rbrv_image_anchor} declarations that {rbtgo_ark_conjure} does — so an enshrined vessel built via kludge consumes the same GAR-anchored base layers as the conjure path — but anchored layers must be pre-cached locally; the credentialed fetch happens out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin-only slots (no anchor) require no credentials, so kludge pulls them inline if they're not already cached.

NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.

NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.

The kludge operation:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtga_ark}
. *Validate Input Parameters*:
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)

//axhos_step
. *Resolve Base Images*:
.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
{rbrv_image_origin}
is set:
... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is also set, resolve the slot's `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg to the full GAR reference composed from the locator (`«REGISTRY_HOST»/«PROJECT»/«REPOSITORY»/«PACKAGE_PATH»:«TAG»`) — same shape conjure produces
... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is empty, pass {rbrv_image_origin} through verbatim as the build-arg
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if a slot has {rbrv_image_anchor} set but {rbrv_image_origin} empty: "Malformed regime — anchor without origin"

//axhos_step
. *Verify Local Image Presence*:
.. For each resolved slot ref, query the local container runtime (`docker image inspect`) to confirm the image is cached locally
.. On pass-through miss (origin without anchor): announce and run `docker pull «origin»` inline, then re-inspect to confirm the pull landed an image locally. No credentials are required for public upstream registries.
.. On anchored miss: accumulate the missed locator for post-loop refusal
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if any anchored miss accumulated, emit a remediation block pointing at {rbtgo_image_retrieve} for each missed locator and abort before invoking docker build
.. The credentialed fetch for anchored slots is deliberately out-of-band — kludge never authenticates against GAR. The inline origin pull reaches upstream without credentials and does not change this property.

//axhos_step
. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
.. {rbbc_store}
`«HALLMARK»`

//axhos_step
. *Build Image Locally*:
.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
.. The no-network property comes from the presence check above (every slot verified in the local cache — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent) combined with buildx's default behavior of not re-pulling base images. Hardcoded `FROM` stages that bypass `RBF_IMAGE_n` parameterization remain a residual risk; buildx no longer exposes a `--pull=never` analog, so explicit assertion of the no-network contract at build time is no longer available
.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format

//axhos_step
. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree (`«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`)
.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)

//axhos_step
. *Report Hallmark*:
.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining

== Limitations

* Requires GCP depot configuration to construct GAR-formatted image tags (registry host, project, repository), but no credentials are consumed and no registry is contacted for hallmark publication — only origin-slot upstream pulls reach a registry, unauthenticated
* Anchored base images must be cached locally before kludge runs — fetched out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin pass-through slots auto-pull inline if missing, so the cold-cache path for origin-only vessels is a single operator step; vessels with anchored slots remain two steps (wrest, then kludge).
* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
* No about artifact, no provenance chain -- the vouch alias carries no attestation content
* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks

== Tabtarget

`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`

== See Also

* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build; shares anchor-resolution semantics with kludge
* {rbtgo_image_retrieve} -- credentialed wrest of an enshrined base image into the local cache (remediation target when an anchored slot is missing)
* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)
