content.text: Fast suite green at 98/98. Now an empirical check of `rbfh_dockerfile_check` against all five vessel Dockerfiles to confirm hygiene passes post-remediation.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/rbfh_smoke.sh <<'SMOKE'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
# Minimal harness: stub BUC primitives, then exercise rbfh_dockerfile_check
buc_die()      { echo "DIE: $*" >&2; exit 1; }
buc_log_args() { :; }
BURD_TEMP_DIR=/tmp
source /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh
zrbfh_kindle
for f in \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-forge-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-sentry-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/build-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-busybox/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether; do
  if rbfh_dockerfile_check "$f"; then
    echo "PASS: $f"
  else
    echo "FAIL: $f"
    exit 1
  fi
done

echo "---"
echo "Now negative-test with a synthetic hardcoded FROM..."
TMP=$(mktemp)
cat > "$TMP" <<EOFDF
# A comment
FROM python:3.12-slim
RUN echo hi
EOFDF
if rbfh_dockerfile_check "$TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "BUG: hardcoded FROM should have been refused"
  exit 1
else
  echo "PASS (negative): hardcoded FROM refused as expected"
fi
rm -f "$TMP"

echo "---"
echo "Negative tests for tab + trailing backslash..."
TMP=$(mktemp)
printf "FROM\t\${RBF_IMAGE_1}\n" > "$TMP"
if rbfh_dockerfile_check "$TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "BUG: tab should have been refused"; exit 1
else
  echo "PASS (negative): tab in FROM refused"
fi
printf 'FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} \\\n' > "$TMP"
if rbfh_dockerfile_check "$TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "BUG: trailing backslash should have been refused"; exit 1
else
  echo "PASS (negative): trailing backslash refused"
fi
rm -f "$TMP"
echo "ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED"
SMOKE
bash /tmp/rbfh_smoke.sh
input.description: Smoke-test rbfh against real vessel Dockerfiles plus negative cases

cat > /tmp/rbfh_smoke.sh <<'SMOKE'
#!/bin/bash
set -uo pipefail
# Minimal harness: stub BUC primitives, then exercise rbfh_dockerfile_check
buc_die()      { echo "DIE: $*" >&2; exit 1; }
buc_log_args() { :; }
BURD_TEMP_DIR=/tmp
source /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh
zrbfh_kindle

check_pass() {
  local f="$1"
  if ( rbfh_dockerfile_check "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 ); then
    echo "PASS: $f"
  else
    echo "FAIL: $f"; exit 1
  fi
}

check_fail() {
  local f="$1" label="$2" expected_pattern="$3"
  local out
  out=$( rbfh_dockerfile_check "$f" 2>&1 ) && {
    echo "BUG ($label): should have been refused"; exit 1
  }
  if [[ "$out" == *"$expected_pattern"* ]]; then
    echo "PASS (negative): $label refused — ${out##*DIE: }"
  else
    echo "BUG ($label): refused but message lacks pattern '$expected_pattern' — got: $out"
    exit 1
  fi
}

for f in \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-forge-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-sentry-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/build-context/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/rbev-busybox/Dockerfile \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap \
  /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether; do
  check_pass "$f"
done

echo "---"
echo "Negative tests..."

TMP=$(mktemp)
printf '# Some comment\nFROM python:3.12-slim\nRUN echo hi\n' > "$TMP"
check_fail "$TMP" "hardcoded FROM" "FROM image token must be"

printf 'FROM\t${RBF_IMAGE_1}\n' > "$TMP"
check_fail "$TMP" "tab in FROM" "contains tab character"

printf 'FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} \\\n' > "$TMP"
check_fail "$TMP" "trailing backslash" "ends with backslash"

# Multi-stage AS coda should pass
printf 'FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} AS builder\nFROM scratch\nCOPY --from=builder /app /app\n' > "$TMP"
check_pass "$TMP"

# Empty file should pass silently
> "$TMP"
check_pass "$TMP"

# File with only comments
printf '# just a comment\n# another\n' > "$TMP"
check_pass "$TMP"

rm -f "$TMP"
echo "ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED"
SMOKE
bash /tmp/rbfh_smoke.sh

message.content: I'm now wondering if we ought to create a set of theurge tests for this?

content.text:
Good instinct — `rbfh_dockerfile_check` is exactly the right shape for theurge coverage: pure function, deterministic verdict, no I/O beyond file reads. The smoke test I just deleted covered 7 real Dockerfiles + 6 synthetic cases (3 refusals, 3 admissible patterns); that exact surface belongs in `fast` as a regression guard.

The natural parallel is `regime-validation` — pure-bash field validators with positive/negative cases. A `dockerfile-hygiene` fixture would slot alongside it. The real-vessel cases would also auto-catch any future drift in shared Dockerfiles (e.g., if someone hardcodes `FROM` again in `common-sentry-context/`).

**My recommendation: chivvy as a new pace right after BBABI wraps**, not fold it in. Two reasons:

1. **Scope hygiene.** BBABI's docket is complete and tested by the rule-check this pace produced; adding a fixture is additive load-bearing work that deserves its own slate so the wrap-time commit message stays focused.

2. **Fit with the heat.** ₣BB is rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification — adding fast-suite regression coverage for a hygiene contract is exactly the heat's animating purpose. The fixture earns release-gate citizenship.

The cost is modest but real: author Rust cases (`rbtdrf_dh_*` and `rbtdtf_dh_*`), register in the manifest, decide whether negative-case Dockerfile bodies live inline in the Rust source or as fixture files. ~30-60 min of mechanical authoring.

Two questions before I act:

questions.question: Where should the hygiene fixture land?

options.label: New pace chivvied after BBABI
options.description: Slate a fresh pace (e.g. dockerfile-hygiene-fixture) at the head of ≃BB; wrap BBABI first; then mount the new one. Cleanest scope discipline.

options.label: Fold into BBABI before wrapping
options.description: Extend BBABI's scope to include the fixture. Single commit body; simpler ledger but the docket no longer matches what shipped.

options.label: Itch — defer, capture as future work
options.description: Add to jji_itch.md for later prioritization; wrap BBABI as-is. Right answer if other heat work has higher urgency.

questions.question: Should the pristine kludge sentry test still run as part of BBABI's verification?

options.label: Yes — run before wrap
options.description: Notch the work, run tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor end-to-end, confirm hygiene check fires in the conjure path, then wrap. Honors the docket's verification list.

options.label: Skip — fast suite + smoke covers it
options.description: Smoke test already exercised every rule branch; the kludge sentry test would mostly verify the wiring (no hygiene violations expected). Save the time.

content.text:
Investigation complete. Here's how the analogous fast fixtures reach their targets:

**Existing pattern** (from `rbtdrf_fast.rs`): Rust builds a `bash -c` script string that sources the target bash module(s), calls kindles, runs the assertion. Exit code 0/non-zero is the verdict. No CLI involved. The trace is saved per-case.

- `enrollment-validation` cases source `Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh` directly, then exercise `buv_*` functions.
- `regime-validation` cases source 7 modules (buv + rbcc_Constants + rbgc_Constants + rbrr_regime + rbrv_regime + rbrn_regime + rbdc_DerivedConstants), kindle, then exercise validation.

So Rust→bash reach is "source the module, call the function, check exit". The fixture data (regime fragments) lives as **inline Rust string constants**, not on-disk fixture files.

**Three reach options for `rbfh_dockerfile_check`:**

**Option A — Source rbfh directly** (mirrors enrollment-validation)
```
script:  source buc + rbfh, zrbfh_kindle, rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>
data:    Dockerfile bodies as Rust string constants → temp files in dir
```
Pros: matches existing pattern exactly; no new infrastructure; library-module stays library-shaped.
Cons: Needs to source `buc_command.sh` (which expects `BURD_TEMP_DIR` etc.) OR stub `buc_die`/`buc_log_args`. My smoke test proved stubbing works — three lines.

**Option B — Add a thin `rbfh_cli.sh` + tabtarget** so theurge invokes via the CLI
```
script:  tt/rbw-fHc.HygieneCheck.sh <path>
```
Pros: full dispatch+kindle chain exercised; CLI doubles as ad-hoc operator tool.
Cons: ceremony-heavy for a single library function (new CLI, zipper entry, tabtarget, kindle chain) — earns its keep only if operators would want to run hygiene-check standalone.

**Option C — Drive through existing kludge CLI**
```
script:  tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh against a synthetic bad-vessel, expect non-zero
```
Pros: no new scaffolding; exercises wired path.
Cons: kludge has dirty-tree guard + slot resolution + GCP-prefix derivation as upstream — tests become end-to-end rather than unit; can't easily test a fail-on-bad-Dockerfile case without staging a bad vessel.

**My recommendation: Option A**, with two sub-decisions:

1. **Coverage scope** — three plausible widths:
   - *Logic-only*: synthetic Dockerfiles covering each rule branch (pass, fail-tab, fail-literal, fail-backslash, multi-stage-AS, scratch, comment-only, empty). ~8 cases.
   - *Logic + real vessels*: above + iterate all 7 real vessel Dockerfiles and assert pass. Adds drift detection for `common-sentry-context`, `common-ifrit-context` etc. ~15 cases.
   - *Logic + real + RBSFH cross-cases*: above + e.g. spec subdoc references match implementation. Overkill for a hygiene rule.

2. **Dockerfile body source** — inline Rust string constants (matches `regime-validation`'s setup strings) vs. on-disk fixture files (e.g. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/fixtures/dockerfile-hygiene/`). Inline is the established pattern and keeps test surface self-describing.

lastPrompt: I'm now wondering if we ought to create a set of theurge tests for this?

content.text:
Found the precedent: `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` iterates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*` via filesystem glob in bash, runs validation per-vessel inside a single Rust case, returns one aggregate verdict. So theurge already has a "discover and iterate via the regime's own pointer" pattern that doesn't hardcode anything.

That resolves the question. Here's the refined design:

**CLI shape:**
- New `Tools/rbk/rbfh_cli.sh` (BCG CLI for the rbfh module)
- Two public functions:
  - `rbfh_check <dockerfile-path>` — direct path check, no regime dependency
  - `rbfh_check_vessel <vessel-sigil-or-path>` — resolves via existing `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, runs check on `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`; no-arg → list vessels and die helpfully (matches `rbfd_build`'s pattern)

**Tabtargets** (lowercase `h` since hygiene is read-only / local, matching `rbw-fk`, `rbw-fpf`, `rbw-fpc` convention):
- `tt/rbw-fhc.HygieneCheck.sh <dockerfile-path>` — param1 channel
- `tt/rbw-fhv.HygieneCheckVessel.sh <vessel-sigil>` — param1 channel

**Zipper entries** in `rbz_zipper.sh`:
- `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK` → `rbfh_cli.sh` / `rbfh_check`
- `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL` → `rbfh_cli.sh` / `rbfh_check_vessel`

**Theurge fixture `dockerfile-hygiene`** (in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, assigned to fast suite):

- **~8 synthetic cases** — each with a Dockerfile body as an inline Rust string constant; case writes the body to a temp file in `dir`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhc <tempfile>` via `rbtdrf_run_tt`, asserts verdict. Cases:
  - `dh_accept_parameterized` — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` passes
  - `dh_accept_scratch` — `FROM scratch` passes
  - `dh_accept_multistage_as` — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} AS builder\nFROM scratch\n...` passes
  - `dh_accept_empty_dockerfile` — empty file passes silently
  - `dh_accept_comments_only` — only `#`-comments passes silently
  - `dh_reject_hardcoded_literal` — `FROM python:3.12-slim` refused
  - `dh_reject_tab_in_from` — `FROM\t${RBF_IMAGE_1}` refused
  - `dh_reject_trailing_backslash` — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} \` refused

- **1 all-vessels case** — `dh_all_vessels_pass`. Bash script iterates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*` (matching `rbrv_all_vessels` pattern), sources each `rbrv.env` in a subshell to extract `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE` + `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhv <sigil>` for each conjure-mode vessel, expects all to pass. Bash iteration honors "no hardcoded paths in theurge"; vessel-mode tabtarget gets exercised; one case for the aggregate.

Total **~9 new cases** in fast tier (already 98 → 107).

**Other touches:**
- CLAUDE.md mappings: RBFH already added; add a note that `rbfh_cli.sh` is the CLI counterpart.
- Generate updated tabtarget context via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh`.

One question before I shape the docket:

questions.question: Does the design hold, or is there a better way you see?

options.label: Design holds — write the docket
options.description: Two tabtargets (path-mode + vessel-mode), CLI handles both, theurge fixture uses path-mode per synthetic case and bash-iteration with vessel-mode for the all-vessels case. Proceed.

options.label: Drop the path-mode tabtarget
options.description: Operators can ad-hoc check by passing a Dockerfile path inside their vessel via rbw-fhv. Theurge could still test synthetic cases by writing a fake vessel directory in a temp location. Lighter operator surface, slightly heavier theurge setup.

options.label: Drop the vessel-mode tabtarget
options.description: Just path-mode. Theurge enumerates vessels via filesystem glob (mirroring rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels) and invokes path-mode tabtarget per-vessel. Simpler operator surface; loses the 'check this whole vessel by sigil' convenience.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture

## Character

Mechanical: mint a thin BCG CLI + two tabtargets for the `rbfh` module landed in `₢BBABI`, then add a ~9-case theurge fixture in the fast tier for regression coverage of the Dockerfile hygiene contract. Patterns follow `regime-validation` for both reach (path-mode tabtarget per synthetic case, single all-vessels case with bash-side iteration) and shape (inline Rust string constants for synthetic Dockerfile bodies, mirroring `rbtdrf_rv_*` setup strings).

## Mint

- `Tools/rbk/rbfh_cli.sh` — BCG CLI paired with the existing `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` library module (sourced therein). Kindle chain loads buc + rbfc (FoundryCore — needed for vessel resolution) + rbrr regime (for `RBRR_VESSEL_DIR`) + rbrv regime + rbfh. No credentials, no GCP machinery.
- Public functions:
  - `rbfh_check <dockerfile-path>` — direct path check; wraps `rbfh_dockerfile_check` after sentinel.
  - `rbfh_check_vessel <vessel-sigil-or-path>` — resolves via `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, sources vessel rbrv.env, asserts conjure-mode (refuses bind/graft with operator-actionable message), runs check on `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`. No-arg → list available vessels and die helpfully (match `rbfd_build`'s pattern at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1178`).
- Tabtargets (both param1 channel; lowercase `h` since hygiene is read-only/local, paralleling `rbw-fpf`/`rbw-fpc`):
  - `tt/rbw-fhc.HygieneCheck.sh` → `rbfh_check`
  - `tt/rbw-fhv.HygieneCheckVessel.sh` → `rbfh_check_vessel`
- Zipper entries in `rbz_zipper.sh`: `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK` and `RBZ_HYGIENE_CHECK_VESSEL`.

## Fixture

- New theurge fixture `dockerfile-hygiene` defined in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, assigned to the fast suite.
- Synthetic cases (~8): Dockerfile bodies as inline Rust `&'static str` constants; each case writes the body to a temp file in the case's `dir`, invokes `tt/rbw-fhc <tempfile>` via `rbtdrf_run_tt`, asserts verdict. Coverage:
  - `dh_accept_parameterized` (positive — `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`)
  - `dh_accept_scratch` (positive — `FROM scratch`)
  - `dh_accept_multistage_as` (positive — `AS builder` coda admissible)
  - `dh_accept_empty` (positive — empty file passes silently)
  - `dh_accept_comments_only` (positive — column-0 `#` lines only)
  - `dh_reject_hardcoded_literal` (negative — `FROM python:3.12-slim`)
  - `dh_reject_tab_in_from` (negative — tab anywhere on FROM line)
  - `dh_reject_trailing_backslash` (negative — line ends with `\`)
- One all-vessels case `dh_all_vessels_pass`: bash script iterates `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*` (mirroring `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` at `rbtdrf_fast.rs:967`), sources each `rbrv.env` in a subshell to read `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`, skips non-conjure modes, invokes `tt/rbw-fhv <sigil>` per conjure vessel, fails on any non-zero. No hardcoded vessel paths in theurge — discovery flows through the regime's own pointer.

## Touches

- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: extend the RBFH entry to mention the CLI counterpart, or add a sibling `rbfh_cli.sh` line.
- Regenerate `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` so the two new tabtargets appear in the Foundry table.
- No spec edits required: `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` describes the rule itself, not the test surface. `RBSAK`/`RBSAC` references to `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` already landed in `₢BBABI`.

## Verification

- `bash -n` clean on `rbfh_cli.sh` and any touched modules.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (case count grows ~98 → ~107).
- Sanity-check: temporarily flip one synthetic case's `expect_ok` flag, confirm the fixture reports the case as failed (verdict assertion is wired correctly), then revert.

## Out of scope

- Handbook prose for the new tabtargets — operators discover via `ls tt/` plus context. Chivvy a doc itch if drift becomes noticeable.
- Spec subdoc edits to RBSFH for the test surface — fixture is implementation detail, not contract.
- Decomposing `rbfh_check_vessel`'s no-arg list-and-die into a separate `rbfh_list_vessels` function — premature factoring.

## Silks

Current proposal `rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture` fits if `cli` and `fixture` both feel load-bearing at mount time. If just one (probably the fixture) ends up doing most of the work, consider relabeling.

input.params: {"identity": "BBABI", "files": ["CLAUDE.md", "Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbfk_kludge.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbfk_cli.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbfd_cli.sh", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc", "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile", "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/rbrv.env"], "intent": "Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene library module, wire it as a precondition into both kludge (rbfk_kludge) and conjure (rbfd_build) paths, fold BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in rbfk_kludge, remediate the rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization, mint the RBSFH spec subdoc as the canonical rule home, and synchronize RBSAK Step 6 + RBSAC Step 1 + CLAUDE.md acronym mappings. Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<\"${z_line}\") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. CLI furnishings: both rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh now source rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (inserted between rbgu_Utility.sh and the module-specific source) and invoke zrbfh_kindle in their kindle chains; minimal kindle has no constant or scratch-file dependencies, so the addition is purely additive ordering. Callsite wiring — rbfk_kludge.sh: rbfh_dockerfile_check invoked after the anchored-misses guard completes and before the hallmark mint, in a buc_step \"Validating Dockerfile hygiene\" block at the natural conceptual position (post slot-resolution, pre docker-build). rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: identical pattern inserted immediately after the existing test -d \"${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}\" validation in rbfd_build (line 1198 → 1200 gap), before buc_info \"Building vessel image\" — fail-fast before any GAR/credential/Cloud Build cost incurs on a malformed Dockerfile. BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge.sh (BBABH debt fold-in): (a) ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX readonly kindle constant defined as \"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_\" plus mutable z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0 mutable kindle state in zrbfk_kindle; (b) new zrbfk_image_present_predicate sentinel'd wrapper around docker image inspect that increments the counter, routes stderr to ${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${counter} forensic temp file, and returns docker's exit status — three previous \"docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null\" sites (anchored-miss check, origin-miss check, origin re-inspect after pull) all routed through the predicate; (c) ten multi-var local declarations from the slot-resolution loop split one-per-line (z_slot, z_origin_var, z_anchor_var, z_origin, z_anchor, z_slot_ref, z_pkg_path, z_tag, z_miss, all separate local statements); (d) two `test ${#array[@]} -gt 0` sites converted to `(( ${#array[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom for build_args presence + anchored_misses presence. Vessel remediation — rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile: replaced the hardcoded `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` with the standard pattern `# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)\\nARG RBF_IMAGE_1\\nFROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching rbev-busybox and common-sentry-context style — the rbrv.env already had RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm set so the build-arg propagation was always wired; the Dockerfile just ignored it. Empirically verified by surveying all 7 conjure-mode vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge-context, common-sentry-context, jupyter, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether): every one now uses ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} or AS coda variants. Also tidied rbrv.env: removed a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived AFTER the `# eof` marker — pre-existing cleanup matching the shape of working conjure vessels like rbev-bottle-ccyolo which omit the empty ANCHOR entirely. Spec subdoc RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc minted in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/: follows RBSDS-dns_step shape (//axhob_operation header with quoin reference, prose intro establishing scope across kludge and conjure callsites, three //axhos_step blocks one per rule), MCM-annotated, uses linked terms {rbtgo_ark_kludge}, {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, {rbbc_fatal}, {rbrv_image_origin}, {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} all verified present in mapping section. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc updated in two places: (1) mapping section gained a new \"// Foundry Internals (build-side hygiene contracts shared across modes)\" block with the single quoin `:rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene: <<rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene,Dockerfile Hygiene>>` immediately after the Script Internals block (line ~313); (2) new top-level section `== Foundry Internals` inserted between `== Script Internals` and `== Trade Studies` (line ~4120), containing the anchor block `[[rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene]]` + `//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal` annotation + `=== {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` header + 5-line intro + `include::RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc[]`. RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Step 6 (Build Image Locally) reworked: replaced BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM stages being a residual risk with a new leading bullet calling out the hygiene validation as the precondition (\"Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch...\") and reframed the no-network paragraph to credit {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} for closing the residual hardcoded-FROM risk that --pull=never used to backstop. RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc Step 1 (Validate Input Parameters) gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet — \"vessel Dockerfile passes {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} — any column-0 FROM line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch as its image token; hardcoded literals abort before {gcb_service} submission\". CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: added RBFH entry under RBK Subdirectory pointing at rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh with parenthetical description \"(Foundry Hygiene — Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure)\", slotted between RBF and RBGA in the existing alphabetical block; added RBSFH entry pointing at rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc between RBSDY and RBSGD in the spec subsection. Verification — bash -n clean on all five touched bash modules (rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh, rbfk_kludge.sh, rbfk_cli.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfd_cli.sh) confirmed individually; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green at 98/98 across enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (27), regime-smoke (9), handbook-render (15) — handbook-render in particular exercises asciidoctor on the full rendered handbook through the RBS0 include chain, validating the new RBSFH subdoc syntax + the RBSAK/RBSAC edits render cleanly; standalone smoke test of rbfh_dockerfile_check authored against a minimal harness (stubbed buc_die/buc_log_args, kindle, then check against all 7 real conjure-vessel Dockerfiles plus 6 synthetic positive/negative cases: hardcoded literal → refused, tab in FROM → refused, trailing backslash → refused, multi-stage AS coda → admitted, empty file → admitted, comment-only file → admitted) — all expected verdicts produced with the exact `path:lineno: rule — offending line` diagnostic format spec'd by the docket. Departures from docket: minor only — (a) docket said \"verify field name [RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN] against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing\", verified empirically (ccyolo, busybox, etc.) and the jupyter rbrv.env already had the correct field set so the field-name verification step was a no-op (the bypass was purely in the Dockerfile); (b) the rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` line removal in jupyter rbrv.env was opportunistic cleanup not explicitly docketed but matches the shape of working conjure vessels and was clearly broken (positioned after `# eof`). Pristine kludge sentry verification (tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor) deferred to the post-notch step per the kludge dirty-tree guard; will run against this commit's state. Open BCG debt: none surfaced during the rbfk fold-in beyond what was explicitly listed in the docket. Theurge fixture coverage for the rbfh hygiene-check function is chivvied as ₢BBABJ (rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture pace cantled after this one) to keep BBABI's scope focused on the contract and its initial callsite wiring; BBABJ will add the operator-facing CLI + tabtargets + ~9-case fast-tier fixture for regression protection."}

RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Step 6 (Build Image Locally) reworked: replaced BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM stages being a residual risk with a new leading bullet calling out the hygiene validation as the precondition ("Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch...") and reframed the no-network paragraph to credit {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} for closing the residual hardcoded-FROM risk that --pull=never used to backstop. RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc Step 1 (Validate Input Parameters) gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet — "vessel Dockerfile passes {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} — any column-0 FROM line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch as its image token; hardcoded literals abort before {gcb_service} submission". CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: added RBFH entry under RBK Subdirectory pointing at rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh with parenthetical description "(Foundry Hygiene — Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure)", slotted between RBF and RBGA in the existing alphabetical block; added RBSFH entry pointing at rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc between RBSDY and RBSGD in the spec subsection. Verification — bash -n clean on all five touched bash modules (rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh, rbfk_kludge.sh, rbfk_cli.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfd_cli.sh) confirmed individually; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green at 98/98 across enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (27), regime-smoke (9), handbook-render (15) — handbook-render in particular exercises asciidoctor on the full rendered handbook through the RBS0 include chain, validating the new RBSFH subdoc syntax + the RBSAK/RBSAC edits render cleanly; standalone smoke test of rbfh_dockerfile_check authored against a minimal harness (stubbed buc_die/buc_log_args, kindle, then check against all 7 real conjure-vessel Dockerfiles plus 6 synthetic positive/negative cases: hardcoded literal → refused, tab in FROM → refused, trailing backslash → refused, multi-stage AS coda → admitted, empty file → admitted, comment-only file → admitted) — all expected verdicts produced with the exact `path:lineno: rule — offending line` diagnostic format spec'd by the docket. Departures from docket: minor only — (a) docket said "verify field name [RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN] against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing", verified empirically (ccyolo, busybox, etc.) and the jupyter rbrv.env already had the correct field set so the field-name verification step was a no-op (the bypass was purely in the Dockerfile); (b) the rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` line removal in jupyter rbrv.env was opportunistic cleanup not explicitly docketed but matches the shape of working conjure vessels and was clearly broken (positioned after `# eof`). Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. file.content:
source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbob_bottle.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rboo_observe.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  if test -z "${z_folio}"; then
    local z_monikers
    z_monikers=$(rbrn_list_capture) || buc_die "No nameplates found"
    buc_step "Available nameplates:"
    local z_moniker=""
    for z_moniker in ${z_monikers}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_moniker}"
    done
    buc_die "Nameplate moniker required (pass as argument)"
  fi
  local z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_folio}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  zrbrn_kindle

  # Differential enforce: nameplate-keyed kludge commands write
  # RBRN_*_HALLMARK, so zrbrn_enforce would self-veto on marshal-zero
  # state where those fields are blank by design (rblm zeroes them;
  # rbtdrp_pristine treats them as RBTDRP_RBRN_BLANK_FIELDS). Strict
  # validation lives in rbw-rnv (rbrn_cli.sh). Mirrors the yoke/RBRV
  # split (rbfl_cli vs rbrv_cli). rbob_ordain (rbw-tO) keeps enforce —
  # it is the nameplate-keyed gate before incurring Cloud Build spend;
  # the foundry-keyed ordain (rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain) is vessel-only and
  # never enters this CLI, so onboarding-sequence's ordain steps are
  # unaffected.
  case "${z_command}" in
    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry)
      ;;
    *)
      zrbrn_enforce
      ;;
  esac

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbob_kindle

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBOB CLI - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle command-line interface
#
# Commands:
#   start           Start crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle)
#   stop            Stop crucible
#   hail            Hail sentry (interactive shell)
#   rack            Rack bottle (interactive shell)
#   scry            Scry network traffic (tcpdump)
#   info            Show container names, network, and runtime
#   validate        Validate configuration is complete

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# CLI Commands

rbob_validate() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Validate that RBOB configuration is complete and runnable"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "RBOB Validate: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # All values computed at kindle - just verify they exist
  test -n "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_RUNTIME not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_SENTRY not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_PENTACLE not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_BOTTLE not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_NETWORK not set"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Sentry script not found: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Pentacle script not found: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Compose base not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  buc_step "RBOB configuration valid"
  echo "Moniker:       ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
  echo "Runtime:       ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
  echo "Sentry:        ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  echo "Pentacle:        ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  echo "Bottle:        ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  echo "Network:       ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
  echo "Compose base:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
  echo "Compose frag:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT} ($(test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}" && echo 'exists' || echo 'not found'))"
}

rbob_info() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Show container names, network, and runtime for kindled nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "RBOB Info: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
  echo "Runtime:   ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
  echo "Sentry:    ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  echo "Pentacle:    ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  echo "Bottle:    ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  echo "Network:   ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
  echo "Sentry IP: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
  echo "Bottle IP: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
}

rbob_scry() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Scry network traffic on crucible containers"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Kindle observe module and delegate
  zrboo_kindle
  rboo_observe
}

rbob_charged() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  rbob_charged_predicate
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbob_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BUZ_FOLIO             " "Nameplate moniker (e.g., tadmor)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_command="${1:-}"

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrn_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbob_bottle.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rboo_observe.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  if test -z "${z_folio}"; then
    local z_monikers
    z_monikers=$(rbrn_list_capture) || buc_die "No nameplates found"
    buc_step "Available nameplates:"
    local z_moniker=""
    for z_moniker in ${z_monikers}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_moniker}"
    done
    buc_die "Nameplate moniker required (pass as argument)"
  fi
  local z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_folio}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  zrbrn_kindle

  # Differential enforce: nameplate-keyed kludge commands write
  # RBRN_*_HALLMARK, so zrbrn_enforce would self-veto on marshal-zero
  # state where those fields are blank by design (rblm zeroes them;
  # rbtdrp_pristine treats them as RBTDRP_RBRN_BLANK_FIELDS). Strict
  # validation lives in rbw-rnv (rbrn_cli.sh). Mirrors the yoke/RBRV
  # split (rbfl_cli vs rbrv_cli). rbob_ordain (rbw-tO) keeps enforce —
  # it is the nameplate-keyed gate before incurring Cloud Build spend;
  # the foundry-keyed ordain (rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain) is vessel-only and
  # never enters this CLI, so onboarding-sequence's ordain steps are
  # unaffected.
  case "${z_command}" in
    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry)
      ;;
    *)
      zrbrn_enforce
      ;;
  esac

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbob_kindle

  # Differential kindle: kludge uses uncredentialed rbfk; ordain needs full rbfd
  case "${z_command}" in
    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry)
      zrbfk_kindle
      ;;
    rbob_ordain)
      zrbfd_kindle
      ;;
  esac

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbob_ "Recipe Bottle Orchestration" zrbob_furnish "$@"

# eof

input.params: {"identity": "BBABI", "files": ["Tools/rbk/rbob_cli.sh"], "intent": "Wire rbfh into rbob_cli.sh — wiring miss surfaced empirically by the post-notch kludge sentry verification. The original BBABI notch added rbfh source + zrbfh_kindle to rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh, but missed rbob_cli.sh which is the actual operator entry point for tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh (routes via rbob_kludge_sentry → rbfk_kludge → rbfh_dockerfile_check). Empirical signature: kludge sentry got past origin-slot auto-pull (debian:bookworm-slim pulled fresh, presence guard cleared), hit the new buc_step 'Validating Dockerfile hygiene', then failed with 'rbfh_dockerfile_check: command not found' at rbfk_kludge.sh:192 — function literal exists in rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh but the module wasn't sourced in this CLI's furnish so the symbol was unresolved at call time. Same wiring fix applies for the ordain path too (rbob_ordain → rbfd_build → rbfh_dockerfile_check), which would have surfaced on the next pristine gauntlet run; preempted here. Fix shape mirrors the rbfk_cli/rbfd_cli edits from the parent notch — single source line inserted between rbgo_OAuth.sh and rbob_bottle.sh (alphabetical-ish + dependency-before-consumer per BCG); single zrbfh_kindle call inserted before the differential rbfk/rbfd kindle case so both branches inherit hygiene-kindled state. The hygiene kindle is unconditional (not differential) since both kludge AND ordain commands route through callsites that invoke rbfh_dockerfile_check — a discriminator case would be premature factoring. The buildx output included an InvalidDefaultArgInFrom warning ('Default value for ARG ${RBF_IMAGE_1} results in empty or invalid base image name (line 3)') which is benign and pre-existed our changes — the build correctly resolves ${RBF_IMAGE_1} via the --build-arg passed by rbfk_kludge and the resulting image inspects with the right base layer. The warning is buildx complaining that ARG-without-default RBF_IMAGE_1 has no fallback at parse time, but the actual build evaluates the build-arg and proceeds; no operational impact. Bottle hallmark unchanged (k260513131436-2c1bbbc2 — bottle wasn't touched this iteration). This commit completes BBABI's docketed verification list: bash -n clean (notched), fast suite 98/98 (notched), kludge sentry end-to-end with hygiene gate firing (this commit's empirical proof). Theurge fixture coverage of the hygiene rule space chivvied to ₢BBABJ.
c2f1e90a jjb:1011-4e3c064f:₢BBABI:n: Wire rbfh into rbob_cli.sh — wiring miss surfaced empirically by the post-notch kludge sentry verification. The original BBABI notch added rbfh source + zrbfh_kindle to rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh, but missed rbob_cli.sh which is the actual operator entry point for tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh (routes via rbob_kludge_sentry → rbfk_kludge → rbfh_dockerfile_check). Empirical signature: kludge sentry got past origin-slot auto-pull (debian:bookworm-slim pulled fresh, presence guard cleared), hit the new buc_step 'Validating Dockerfile hygiene', then failed with 'rbfh_dockerfile_check: command not found' at rbfk_kludge.sh:192 — function literal exists in rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh but the module wasn't sourced in this CLI's furnish so the symbol was unresolved at call time. Same wiring fix applies for the ordain path too (rbob_ordain → rbfd_build → rbfh_dockerfile_check), which would have surfaced on the next pristine gauntlet run; preempted here. Fix shape mirrors the rbfk_cli/rbfd_cli edits from the parent notch — single source line inserted between rbgo_OAuth.sh and rbob_bottle.sh (alphabetical-ish + dependency-before-consumer per BCG); single zrbfh_kindle call inserted before the differential rbfk/rbfd kindle case so both branches inherit hygiene-kindled state. The hygiene kindle is unconditional (not differential) since both kludge AND ordain commands route through callsites that invoke rbfh_dockerfile_check — a discriminator case would be premature factoring. Comment added at the kindle site documents this load-bearing-for-both-paths property. Verification — bash -n clean on rbob_cli.sh; kludge sentry re-run follows this notch under the same dirty-tree guard that exposed the issue (the dirty-tree refusal is itself the proof that my fix has been staged correctly, since the test depends on a committed state). Open: pristine kludge sentry against tadmor itself, which after this commit will run from a clean tree as the empirical confirmation that the hygiene gate fires correctly inline.
4e3c064f jjb:1011-935868e0::i: OFFICIUM 260514-1005
935868e0 jjb:1011-66814134:₢BBABI:n: Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene library module, wire it as a precondition into both kludge (rbfk_kludge) and conjure (rbfd_build) paths, fold BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in rbfk_kludge, remediate the rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization, mint the RBSFH spec subdoc as the canonical rule home, and synchronize RBSAK Step 6 + RBSAC Step 1 + CLAUDE.md acronym mappings. Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. CLI furnishings: both rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh now source rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (inserted between rbgu_Utility.sh and the module-specific source) and invoke zrbfh_kindle in their kindle chains; minimal kindle has no constant or scratch-file dependencies, so the addition is purely additive ordering. Callsite wiring — rbfk_kludge.sh: rbfh_dockerfile_check invoked after the anchored-misses guard completes and before the hallmark mint, in a buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene" block at the natural conceptual position (post slot-resolution, pre docker-build). rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: identical pattern inserted immediately after the existing test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" validation in rbfd_build (line 1198 → 1200 gap), before buc_info "Building vessel image" — fail-fast before any GAR/credential/Cloud Build cost incurs on a malformed Dockerfile. BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge.sh (BBABH debt fold-in): (a) ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX readonly kindle constant defined as "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_" plus mutable z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0 mutable kindle state in zrbfk_kindle; (b) new zrbfk_image_present_predicate sentinel'd wrapper around docker image inspect that increments the counter, routes stderr to ${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${counter} forensic temp file, and returns docker's exit status — three previous "docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null" sites (anchored-miss check, origin-miss check, origin re-inspect after pull) all routed through the predicate; (c) ten multi-var local declarations from the slot-resolution loop split one-per-line (z_slot, z_origin_var, z_anchor_var, z_origin, z_anchor, z_slot_ref, z_pkg_path, z_tag, z_miss, all separate local statements); (d) two `test ${#array[@]} -gt 0` sites converted to `(( ${#array[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom for build_args presence + anchored_misses presence. Vessel remediation — rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile: replaced the hardcoded `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` with the standard pattern `# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)\nARG RBF_IMAGE_1\nFROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching rbev-busybox and common-sentry-context style — the rbrv.env already had RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm set so the build-arg propagation was always wired; the Dockerfile just ignored it. Empirically verified by surveying all 7 conjure-mode vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge-context, common-sentry-context, jupyter, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether): every one now uses ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} or AS coda variants. Also tidied rbrv.env: removed a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived AFTER the `# eof` marker — pre-existing cleanup matching the shape of working conjure vessels like rbev-bottle-ccyolo which omit the empty ANCHOR entirely. Spec subdoc RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc minted in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/: follows RBSDS-dns_step shape (//axhob_operation header with quoin reference, prose intro establishing scope across kludge and conjure callsites, three //axhos_step blocks one per rule), MCM-annotated, uses linked terms {rbtgo_ark_kludge}, {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, {rbbc_fatal}, {rbrv_image_origin}, {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} all verified present in mapping section. lastPrompt: notch.  List any remaining concerns about wrapping next first.

content.text:
Tree clean — nothing to notch. Concerns for the wrap decision, in priority order:

**1. Conjure path's hygiene check was not empirically exercised.** Only the kludge path got live verification via `rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor`. The conjure path's call (`rbfd_build` at line 1200) is wired identically — single `rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"` after the existing `test -d` validation — and bash -n + the unit smoke confirm the function works against every existing Dockerfile. But no live Cloud Build run exercises rbfd_build's actual point-of-call. Verification deferred to "whenever the next conjure happens" (a `rbw-fO` against any conjure vessel will validate). Reasonable trade given Cloud Build cost; flag it.

**2. The buildx `InvalidDefaultArgInFrom` warning is now hygiene-mandated noise.** Every kludge/conjure build now emits:
```
WARN: InvalidDefaultArgInFrom: Default value for ARG ${RBF_IMAGE_1} results in empty or invalid base image name (line 3)
```
Pre-existed BBABI, but my hygiene rule mandates exactly this shape (`ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` with no default, `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`). Three resolutions: (a) accept as harmless cosmetic noise — buildx is wrong, the build is correct; (b) extend the hygiene contract to require `ARG RBF_IMAGE_n=<placeholder>` defaults; (c) suppress at the docker build invocation. None blocks BBABI. Worth chivvying as a small itch.

**3. BBABI's effective scope grew empirically beyond the original docket.** Docket said: module + callsites + cleanups + vessel remediation + spec sync. What actually shipped (across 3 notches): all of that + rbob_cli wiring fix (emergent discovery from the kludge sentry test) + opportunistic `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` cleanup in jupyter rbrv.env + nameplate hallmark bump. All four extra touches are documented in their respective notch intents. Operationally honest; just note the wrap message will summarize 3 commits' worth of work, not one.

**Not concerns** (already accounted for):
- Theurge regression coverage deferred to ₢BBABJ — by design, paddock locked.
- Stale `RBF → rbk/rbf_Foundry.sh` entry in CLAUDE.md — pre-existed, not introduced here.
- No edits to RBSCO/cosmology — rule lives at the right abstraction level.

Wrap if you're happy with the above. Or if (1) feels uncomfortable, I can chivvy a "next-conjure verification" itch under ₣BB for someone to notice on the next live conjure pass.

message.content: You created new theurge test cases, correct?  Did you attach them to existing fixtures/suites such that they will be regularly rerun?

content.text:
No — and this is worth being precise about because the distinction matters for regression protection.

**What ran during BBABI verification:**

- **One-off bash smoke harness** at `/tmp/rbfh_smoke.sh` — I wrote it, ran it, then `rm -f`'d it. It sourced `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`, stubbed `buc_die`/`buc_log_args`, kindled, then exercised the function against 7 real Dockerfiles + 6 synthetic cases. **It is gone. Nothing rerunnable.**
- **Fast suite** (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`) — case count unchanged at **98/98** before and after BBABI. The four fixtures (enrollment-validation 47, regime-validation 27, regime-smoke 9, handbook-render 15) caught spec-render syntax problems via `handbook-render`'s asciidoctor pass, but none of them exercise `rbfh_dockerfile_check`.

**What is regression-protected:**

- The **spec rendering** (RBSFH subdoc + RBSAK/RBSAC edits + RBS0 mapping/section additions) — `handbook-render` will fail if anyone breaks the AsciiDoc.
- **Nothing else** about the hygiene contract has a regression test in the standing suite. If someone breaks `rbfh_dockerfile_check`'s rule logic tomorrow, the fast suite stays 98/98 green. The bug would surface only when a kludge or conjure run hits a malformed Dockerfile, or never — depending on whether anyone notices.

**The regression gap is the entire reason BBABJ exists.** When you mounted the question about theurge tests earlier in this session and we chatted about reach, that wasn't a hypothetical — it was the answer to this exact concern. The cantled ₢BBABJ docket commits to:
- `rbfh_cli.sh` + two tabtargets (`rbw-fhc`, `rbw-fhv`) — operator surface
- New `dockerfile-hygiene` fixture in `rbtdrf_fast.rs`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, assigned to **fast** suite
- ~9 cases (8 synthetic per-rule + 1 all-vessels iteration)

After BBABJ wraps, fast tier grows from 98 → ~107 and the hygiene rules are regression-locked.

So: **honest answer is no**, and this is exactly the asymmetry your earlier "should we create theurge tests for this?" instinct was sensing. The protection isn't in BBABI; it's queued in BBABJ.

If you want regression coverage IN this pace before wrapping, the cleanest option is to fold BBABJ into BBABI — drop the cantle, do the fixture work now, single wrap. The trade is scope discipline vs. shipping the contract un-protected. Your call.

Wired as fail-fast precondition into both rbfk_kludge (after slot resolution, before docker build) and rbfd_build (after path validation, before vessel image build); source + zrbfh_kindle threaded through three CLI furnishes (rbfk_cli, rbfd_cli, rbob_cli — the rbob_cli wiring fix was an empirical discovery from the kludge sentry verification, fixed inline as ₢BBABI's second notch). Folded BBABH's BCG debt into rbfk_kludge: split ten multi-var locals one-per-line, converted two `test ${#arr[@]} -gt 0` sites to `(( ${#arr[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom, and extracted zrbfk_image_present_predicate consolidating the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites with counter-discriminated forensic stderr capture via new ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX kindle constant + z_rbfk_inspect_counter mutable state. Remediated rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter (the only vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization): Dockerfile head now uses `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching the rbev-busybox/common-sentry-context style; opportunistically tidied a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived after `# eof` in rbrv.env. Minted RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc as the canonical rule home (follows RBSDS-dns_step shape, MCM-annotated, `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin); registered in RBS0 with a new mapping-section block and `== Foundry Internals` top-level section between Script Internals and Trade Studies. Synchronized cross-references: RBSAK Step 6 reframed around the hygiene gate (removing BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM as residual risk), RBSAC Step 1 gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet, CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings added RBFH (module) and RBSFH (spec subdoc) entries. Empirical verification: bash -n clean on all five touched bash modules; fast suite 98/98 across enrollment-validation (47) + regime-validation (27) + regime-smoke (9) + handbook-render (15), with handbook-render exercising asciidoctor against the new RBSFH subdoc + RBSAK/RBSAC/RBS0 edits via the full include chain; one-off smoke harness exercised rbfh_dockerfile_check against all 7 real conjure-vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge, common-sentry, jupyter post-remediation, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether — all admitted) plus 6 synthetic cases (hardcoded literal, tab in FROM, trailing backslash → refused with exact diagnostic format; multi-stage AS coda, empty file, comment-only file → admitted); end-to-end pristine `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` green against a clean tree, hygiene gate fired inline in the conjure-mirror path, slot-1 debian:bookworm-slim resolved via origin pass-through, buildx-driven docker build produced new hallmark k260514114349-c2f1e90a, vouch tag created, tadmor nameplate drove from k260513212837-ff28ac84 → k260514114349-c2f1e90a. Departures from docket — all empirical and documented in notch intents: (a) rbob_cli rbfh wiring fix was not in original scope but was load-bearing for the actual operator path through tt/rbw-cKS, surfaced cleanly as 'rbfh_dockerfile_check: command not found' on the first kludge sentry attempt; (b) opportunistic rbrv.env cleanup of post-EOF anchor line, matching working-vessel shape; (c) tadmor nameplate hallmark bump as the wrap-time empirical install record. Open: conjure path's hygiene gate is identically wired but was not empirically exercised (live Cloud Build cost gate); will validate on the next live rbw-fO against any conjure vessel. The standing buildx InvalidDefaultArgInFrom warning ('Default value for ARG ${RBF_IMAGE_1} results in empty or invalid base image name') is pre-existing benign noise that the hygiene rule now mandates by construction; chivvy as a small itch if the noise becomes operationally annoying. Theurge regression protection for the hygiene rule space is intentionally deferred to ₢BBABJ (rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture, cantled after this pace) — that pace will mint rbfh_cli.sh + tt/rbw-fhc + tt/rbw-fhv tabtargets and add a ~9-case dockerfile-hygiene fixture to the fast suite (synthetic per-rule cases via path-mode tabtarget + one all-vessels iteration via vessel-mode tabtarget, mirroring rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels). After BBABJ lands, the fast tier grows 98 → ~107 and the rule space is regression-locked."}

c2f1e90a jjb:1011-4e3c064f:₢BBABI:n: Wire rbfh into rbob_cli.sh — wiring miss surfaced empirically by the post-notch kludge sentry verification. Empirical signature: kludge sentry got past origin-slot auto-pull (debian:bookworm-slim pulled fresh, presence guard cleared), hit the new buc_step 'Validating Dockerfile hygiene', then failed with 'rbfh_dockerfile_check: command not found' at rbfk_kludge.sh:192 — function literal exists in rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh but the module wasn't sourced in this CLI's furnish so the symbol was unresolved at call time. Same wiring fix applies for the ordain path too (rbob_ordain → rbfd_build → rbfh_dockerfile_check), which would have surfaced on the next pristine gauntlet run; preempted here. Fix shape mirrors the rbfk_cli/rbfd_cli edits from the parent notch — single source line inserted between rbgo_OAuth.sh and rbob_bottle.sh (alphabetical-ish + dependency-before-consumer per BCG); single zrbfh_kindle call inserted before the differential rbfk/rbfd kindle case so both branches inherit hygiene-kindled state. The hygiene kindle is unconditional (not differential) since both kludge AND ordain commands route through callsites that invoke rbfh_dockerfile_check — a discriminator case would be premature factoring. Comment added at the kindle site documents this load-bearing-for-both-paths property. Verification — bash -n clean on rbob_cli.sh; kludge sentry re-run follows this notch under the same dirty-tree guard that exposed the issue (the dirty-tree refusal is itself the proof that my fix has been staged correctly, since the test depends on a committed state). Open: pristine kludge sentry against tadmor itself, which after this commit will run from a clean tree as the empirical confirmation that the hygiene gate fires correctly inline.

935868e0 jjb:1011-66814134:₢BBABI:n: Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene library module, wire it as a precondition into both kludge (rbfk_kludge) and conjure (rbfd_build) paths, fold BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in rbfk_kludge, remediate the rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization, mint the RBSFH spec subdoc as the canonical rule home, and synchronize RBSAK Step 6 + RBSAC Step 1 + CLAUDE.md acronym mappings. Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. CLI furnishings: both rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh now source rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (inserted between rbgu_Utility.sh and the module-specific source) and invoke zrbfh_kindle in their kindle chains; minimal kindle has no constant or scratch-file dependencies, so the addition is purely additive ordering. Callsite wiring — rbfk_kludge.sh: rbfh_dockerfile_check invoked after the anchored-misses guard completes and before the hallmark mint, in a buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene" block at the natural conceptual position (post slot-resolution, pre docker-build). rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: identical pattern inserted immediately after the existing test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" validation in rbfd_build (line 1198 → 1200 gap), before buc_info "Building vessel image" — fail-fast before any GAR/credential/Cloud Build cost incurs on a malformed Dockerfile. BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge.sh (BBABH debt fold-in): (a) ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX readonly kindle constant defined as "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_" plus mutable z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0 mutable kindle state in zrbfk_kindle; (b) new zrbfk_image_present_predicate sentinel'd wrapper around docker image inspect that increments the counter, routes stderr to ${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${counter} forensic temp file, and returns docker's exit status — three previous "docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null" sites (anchored-miss check, origin-miss check, origin re-inspect after pull) all routed through the predicate; (c) ten multi-var local declarations from the slot-resolution loop split one-per-line (z_slot, z_origin_var, z_anchor_var, z_origin, z_anchor, z_slot_ref, z_pkg_path, z_tag, z_miss, all separate local statements); (d) two `test ${#array[@]} -gt 0` sites converted to `(( ${#array[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom for build_args presence + anchored_misses presence. Vessel remediation — rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile: replaced the hardcoded `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` with the standard pattern `# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)\nARG RBF_IMAGE_1\nFROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching rbev-busybox and common-sentry-context style — the rbrv.env already had RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm set so the build-arg propagation was always wired; the Dockerfile just ignored it. Empirically verified by surveying all 7 conjure-mode vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge-context, common-sentry-context, jupyter, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether): every one now uses ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} or AS coda variants. Also tidied rbrv.env: removed a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived AFTER the `# eof` marker — pre-existing cleanup matching the shape of working conjure vessels like rbev-bottle-ccyolo which omit the empty ANCHOR entirely. Spec subdoc RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc minted in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/: follows RBSDS-dns_step shape (//axhob_operation header with quoin reference, prose intro establishing scope across kludge and conjure callsites, three //axhos_step blocks one per rule), MCM-annotated, uses linked terms {rbtgo_ark_kludge}, {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, {rbbc_fatal}, {rbrv_image_origin}, {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} all verified present in mapping section. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc updated in two places: (1) mapping section gained a new "// Foundry Internals (build-side hygiene contracts shared across modes)" block with the single quoin `:rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene: <<rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene,Dockerfile Hygiene>>` immediately after the Script Internals block (line ~313); (2) new top-level section `== Foundry Internals` inserted between `== Script Internals` and `== Trade Studies` (line ~4120), containing the anchor block `[[rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene]]` + `//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal` annotation + `=== {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` header + 5-line intro + `include::RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc[]`. RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Step 6 (Build Image Locally) reworked: replaced BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM stages being a residual risk with a new leading bullet calling out the hygiene validation as the precondition ("Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch...") and reframed the no-network paragraph to credit {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} for closing the residual hardcoded-FROM risk that --pull=never used to backstop. RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc Step 1 (Validate Input Parameters) gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet — "vessel Dockerfile passes {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} — any column-0 FROM line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch as its image token; hardcoded literals abort before {gcb_service} submission". CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: added RBFH entry under RBK Subdirectory pointing at rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh with parenthetical description "(Foundry Hygiene — Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure)", slotted between RBF and RBGA in the existing alphabetical block; added RBSFH entry pointing at rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc between RBSDY and RBSGD in the spec subsection. Verification — bash -n clean on all five touched bash modules (rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh, rbfk_kludge.sh, rbfk_cli.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfd_cli.sh) confirmed individually; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green at 98/98 across enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (27), regime-smoke (9), handbook-render (15) — handbook-render in particular exercises asciidoctor on the full rendered handbook through the RBS0 include chain, validating the new RBSFH subdoc syntax + the RBSAK/RBSAC edits render cleanly; standalone smoke test of rbfh_dockerfile_check authored against a minimal harness (stubbed buc_die/buc_log_args, kindle, then check against all 7 real conjure-vessel Dockerfiles plus 6 synthetic positive/negative cases: hardcoded literal → refused, tab in FROM → refused, trailing backslash → refused, multi-stage AS coda → admitted, empty file → admitted, comment-only file → admitted) — all expected verdicts produced with the exact `path:lineno: rule — offending line` diagnostic format spec'd by the docket. Departures from docket: minor only — (a) docket said "verify field name [RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN] against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing", verified empirically (ccyolo, busybox, etc.) and the jupyter rbrv.env already had the correct field set so the field-name verification step was a no-op (the bypass was purely in the Dockerfile); (b) the rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` line removal in jupyter rbrv.env was opportunistic cleanup not explicitly docketed but matches the shape of working conjure vessels and was clearly broken (positioned after `# eof`). Pristine kludge sentry verification (tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor) deferred to the post-notch step per the kludge dirty-tree guard; will run against this commit's state. Open BCG debt: none surfaced during the rbfk fold-in beyond what was explicitly listed in the docket. Theurge fixture coverage for the rbfh hygiene-check function is chivvied as ₢BBABJ (rbfh-cli-and-theurge-fixture pace cantled after this one) to keep BBABI's scope focused on the contract and its initial callsite wiring; BBABJ will add the operator-facing CLI + tabtargets + ~9-case fast-tier fixture for regression protection.

c2f1e90a jjb:1011-4e3c064f:₢BBABI:n: Wire rbfh into rbob_cli.sh — wiring miss surfaced empirically by the post-notch kludge sentry verification. The original BBABI notch added rbfh source + zrbfh_kindle to rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh, but missed rbob_cli.sh which is the actual operator entry point for tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh (routes via rbob_kludge_sentry → rbfk_kludge → rbfh_dockerfile_check). Empirical signature: kludge sentry got past origin-slot auto-pull (debian:bookworm-slim pulled fresh, presence guard cleared), hit the new buc_step 'Validating Dockerfile hygiene', then failed with 'rbfh_dockerfile_check: command not found' at rbfk_kludge.sh:192 — function literal exists in rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh but the module wasn't sourced in this CLI's furnish so the symbol was unresolved at call time. Same wiring fix applies for the ordain path too (rbob_ordain → rbfd_build → rbfh_dockerfile_check), which would have surfaced on the next pristine gauntlet run; preempted here. Fix shape mirrors the rbfk_cli/rbfd_cli edits from the parent notch — single source line inserted between rbgo_OAuth.sh and rbob_bottle.sh (alphabetical-ish + dependency-before-consumer per BCG); single zrbfh_kindle call inserted before the differential rbfk/rbfd kindle case so both branches inherit hygiene-kindled state. The hygiene kindle is unconditional (not differential) since both kludge AND ordain commands route through callsites that invoke rbfh_dockerfile_check — a discriminator case would be premature factoring. Comment added at the kindle site documents this load-bearing-for-both-paths property. Verification — bash -n clean on rbob_cli.sh; kludge sentry re-run follows this notch under the same dirty-tree guard that exposed the issue (the dirty-tree refusal is itself the proof that my fix has been staged correctly, since the test depends on a committed state). 935868e0 jjb:1011-66814134:₢BBABI:n: Mint rbfh_FoundryHygiene library module, wire it as a precondition into both kludge (rbfk_kludge) and conjure (rbfd_build) paths, fold BCG cleanups that BBABH accumulated in rbfk_kludge, remediate the rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter vessel that bypassed the RBF_IMAGE_n parameterization, mint the RBSFH spec subdoc as the canonical rule home, and synchronize RBSAK Step 6 + RBSAC Step 1 + CLAUDE.md acronym mappings. Mechanics — new Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (no CLI per docket; CLI deferred to BBABJ for the fixture coverage work): minimal BCG library with ZRBFH_SOURCED guard, zrbfh_kindle/sentinel pair, public rbfh_dockerfile_check(path) that reads the file line-by-line, filters column-0 # comments, matches column-0 FROM lines via [[ =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]], and applies three case-pattern rules with explicit buc_die emitting {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision; rule 1 catches any tab character on a FROM line, rule 2 verifies the second whitespace-delimited token (read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}") is exactly ${RBF_IMAGE_1}, ${RBF_IMAGE_2}, ${RBF_IMAGE_3}, or scratch (lowercase), rule 3 refuses lines ending in `\` literal — multi-stage AS <name> codas after token 2 are admissible by design (rule constrains token 2 only), empty Dockerfiles and comment-only files pass silently per docket. CLI furnishings: both rbfk_cli.sh and rbfd_cli.sh now source rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh (inserted between rbgu_Utility.sh and the module-specific source) and invoke zrbfh_kindle in their kindle chains; minimal kindle has no constant or scratch-file dependencies, so the addition is purely additive ordering. Callsite wiring — rbfk_kludge.sh: rbfh_dockerfile_check invoked after the anchored-misses guard completes and before the hallmark mint, in a buc_step "Validating Dockerfile hygiene" block at the natural conceptual position (post slot-resolution, pre docker-build). rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: identical pattern inserted immediately after the existing test -d "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" validation in rbfd_build (line 1198 → 1200 gap), before buc_info "Building vessel image" — fail-fast before any GAR/credential/Cloud Build cost incurs on a malformed Dockerfile. BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge.sh (BBABH debt fold-in): (a) ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX readonly kindle constant defined as "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfk_inspect_stderr_" plus mutable z_rbfk_inspect_counter=0 mutable kindle state in zrbfk_kindle; (b) new zrbfk_image_present_predicate sentinel'd wrapper around docker image inspect that increments the counter, routes stderr to ${ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX}${counter} forensic temp file, and returns docker's exit status — three previous "docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null" sites (anchored-miss check, origin-miss check, origin re-inspect after pull) all routed through the predicate; (c) ten multi-var local declarations from the slot-resolution loop split one-per-line (z_slot, z_origin_var, z_anchor_var, z_origin, z_anchor, z_slot_ref, z_pkg_path, z_tag, z_miss, all separate local statements); (d) two `test ${#array[@]} -gt 0` sites converted to `(( ${#array[@]} ))` arithmetic-context idiom for build_args presence + anchored_misses presence. Vessel remediation — rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/Dockerfile: replaced the hardcoded `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` with the standard pattern `# Base image resolved by Foundry (anchored GAR ref or upstream pass-through)\nARG RBF_IMAGE_1\nFROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}` matching rbev-busybox and common-sentry-context style — the rbrv.env already had RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm set so the build-arg propagation was always wired; the Dockerfile just ignored it. Empirically verified by surveying all 7 conjure-mode vessel Dockerfiles (common-ifrit-forge-context, common-sentry-context, jupyter, ccyolo, busybox, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap, common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether): every one now uses ARG RBF_IMAGE_1 + FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1} or AS coda variants. Also tidied rbrv.env: removed a rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` empty-value line that lived AFTER the `# eof` marker — pre-existing cleanup matching the shape of working conjure vessels like rbev-bottle-ccyolo which omit the empty ANCHOR entirely. Spec subdoc RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc minted in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/: follows RBSDS-dns_step shape (//axhob_operation header with quoin reference, prose intro establishing scope across kludge and conjure callsites, three //axhos_step blocks one per rule), MCM-annotated, uses linked terms {rbtgo_ark_kludge}, {rbtgo_ark_conjure}, {rbbc_fatal}, {rbrv_image_origin}, {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} all verified present in mapping section. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc updated in two places: (1) mapping section gained a new "// Foundry Internals (build-side hygiene contracts shared across modes)" block with the single quoin `:rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene: <<rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene,Dockerfile Hygiene>>` immediately after the Script Internals block (line ~313); (2) new top-level section `== Foundry Internals` inserted between `== Script Internals` and `== Trade Studies` (line ~4120), containing the anchor block `[[rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene]]` + `//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_internal` annotation + `=== {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` header + 5-line intro + `include::RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc[]`. RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc Step 6 (Build Image Locally) reworked: replaced BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose about hardcoded FROM stages being a residual risk with a new leading bullet calling out the hygiene validation as the precondition ("Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch...") and reframed the no-network paragraph to credit {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} for closing the residual hardcoded-FROM risk that --pull=never used to backstop. RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc Step 1 (Validate Input Parameters) gained a third {rbbc_require} bullet — "vessel Dockerfile passes {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} — any column-0 FROM line must use ${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch as its image token; hardcoded literals abort before {gcb_service} submission". CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: added RBFH entry under RBK Subdirectory pointing at rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh with parenthetical description "(Foundry Hygiene — Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure)", slotted between RBF and RBGA in the existing alphabetical block; added RBSFH entry pointing at rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc between RBSDY and RBSGD in the spec subsection. Verification — bash -n clean on all five touched bash modules (rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh, rbfk_kludge.sh, rbfk_cli.sh, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfd_cli.sh) confirmed individually; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green at 98/98 across enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (27), regime-smoke (9), handbook-render (15) — handbook-render in particular exercises asciidoctor on the full rendered handbook through the RBS0 include chain, validating the new RBSFH subdoc syntax + the RBSAK/RBSAC edits render cleanly; standalone smoke test of rbfh_dockerfile_check authored against a minimal harness (stubbed buc_die/buc_log_args, kindle, then check against all 7 real conjure-vessel Dockerfiles plus 6 synthetic positive/negative cases: hardcoded literal → refused, tab in FROM → refused, trailing backslash → refused, multi-stage AS coda → admitted, empty file → admitted, comment-only file → admitted) — all expected verdicts produced with the exact `path:lineno: rule — offending line` diagnostic format spec'd by the docket. Departures from docket: minor only — (a) docket said "verify field name [RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN] against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing", verified empirically (ccyolo, busybox, etc.) and the jupyter rbrv.env already had the correct field set so the field-name verification step was a no-op (the bypass was purely in the Dockerfile); (b) the rogue `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=` line removal in jupyter rbrv.env was opportunistic cleanup not explicitly docketed but matches the shape of working conjure vessels and was clearly broken (positioned after `# eof`). Pristine kludge sentry verification (tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor) deferred to the post-notch step per the kludge dirty-tree guard; will run against this commit's state.
